q_tail.question: Does the hindwing have a tail?
q_tail.hint: Check the posterior margin of the hindwing for a projection. Most Arhopala tails are at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing is tailed
No — hindwing is tailless

q_tailed_vein3.question: Is the tail short, white-tipped, and located at vein 3?
q_tailed_vein3.hint: In the vast majority of Arhopala the tail (when present) is at vein 2. Only 3 species in the entire genus have a tail at vein 3 — all in the abseus group: A. abseus abseus (small, deep violet-blue male), A. anella (larger, very wide fuscous borders), and A. alesia sacharja (Langkawi; also NE India to Vietnam). The vein 3 tail is one vein closer to the forewing than usual, and is distinctively short and white-tipped. Also check for a spot at the base of hindwing space 1a.

choices.label: Yes — short white-tipped tail at vein 3 (only 3 spp. in entire genus)
No — tail at the usual vein 2 (typical position)

q_tailed_long_tail.question: Is the tail notably long and filamentous — roughly 3.5 mm or more?
q_tailed_long_tail.hint: Two groups have unusually long tails: the corinda group (~3.5 mm) and the aedias group (~6.0 mm). Both are distinctly longer than typical Arhopala tails. Note: A. eumolphus has a tail of only ~3 mm — though it can look relatively long, it does NOT fall in the 'notably long' category. If uncertain or if the tail is broken, choose 'Cannot determine' to continue.

choices.label: Yes — tail is notably long (roughly 3.5–6 mm or more)
No — tail is short and typical
Cannot determine — tail length unclear or broken

q_ijanensis_hw_streak.question: Is there a whitish streak on the hindwing underside running from the base of the dorsum to the apex?
q_ijanensis_hw_streak.hint: Quick diagnostic for A. ijanensis. This species has a distinctive whitish streak on the HW underside running from the base of the dorsum all the way to the apex, with a notably long HW cell (~half wing length or longer). If this streak is visible, the identification is A. ijanensis. If absent, continue with the key.

choices.label: Yes — whitish streak present on HW underside from dorsal base to apex
No — no whitish streak

q_tailed_epimuta.question: Do the underside forewing markings blend into the ground colour with very low contrast — nearly invisible against the background?
q_tailed_epimuta.hint: The epimuta group (A. atosia, A. lurida) is unique among Arhopala in having underside markings that are remarkably faint — the lines and spots barely show against the ground colour. In non-epimuta species the underside markings form clearly-defined lines or spots with moderate to strong contrast. If the underside is not visible, choose 'Cannot determine' — the next step will check the male forewing upperside instead.

choices.label: Yes — underside markings blend in, very low contrast
No — underside markings clearly defined with moderate to strong contrast
Cannot determine — underside not visible

q_tailed_epimuta_upper.question: Does the male forewing upperside have a distinctly differently-coloured or more opaque discal patch?
q_tailed_epimuta_upper.hint: The epimuta group (A. atosia, A. lurida) males have a forewing discal patch that is noticeably more opaque or differently textured than the surrounding shining blue upperside. If neither underside nor upperside is assessable, choose 'Cannot determine' to proceed past this check.

choices.label: Yes — male FW upperside has opaque or differently-coloured discal patch
No
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_tailed_long_dark.question: On the forewing underside, is the basal half of space 1b completely darkened?
q_tailed_long_dark.hint: This is the key difference between the two long-tailed groups. The corinda group has the basal half of FW underside space 1b completely darkened. The aedias group has a long tail (~6.0 mm) but lacks this darkening — the FW underside is normally marked. FW space 1b is often hidden under the hindwing in field shots — if not visible, select Cannot determine and the next question will use tail length instead.

choices.label: Yes — basal half of FW underside space 1b completely darkened (~3.5 mm tail)
No — FW underside normally marked, no darkening (~6.0 mm tail)
Cannot determine — FW space 1b obscured by hindwing

q_long_tail_length.question: What is the underside ground colour and overall appearance?
q_long_tail_length.hint: When FW space 1b is hidden, use these field characters to separate the two long-tailed groups. A. aedias (Large Metallic Oakblue): ground colour hair brown; spots narrowly outlined in pale buff lines; large species (FW 20–29 mm); rounded FW apex; tail ~6 mm. A. corinda: ground colour sometimes glazed with purple wash; large dark patch visible under FW cell when not obscured; FW apex distinctly pointed with straight termen; tail ~3.5 mm. Note also that the tail is sometimes broken in field specimens, making length unreliable. Corinda subspecies: acestes (Malay Peninsula and Singapore), corestes (Langkawi). Aedias subspecies: agnis (standard); meritatas (Langkawi — paler, brighter blue upperside).

choices.label: Hair brown underside; spots narrowly buff-outlined; large species (FW 20–29 mm); rounded FW apex — A. aedias
Underside sometimes with purple wash; dark patch under FW cell; pointed FW apex, straight termen; smaller (tail ~3.5 mm) — A. corinda

q_tailed_ganesa.question: Is this a small species with spots above the forewing cell, a spot at the base of forewing space 3, and the gap between the outer and central spots in hindwing space 7 conspicuously whitened?
q_tailed_ganesa.hint: The ganesa group is characteristically small, with spots visible above the FW cell on the underside, a spot at the base of FW space 3, and short tails. The whitened gap between the outer and central spots in HW space 7 is diagnostic for the whole group and is often the most immediately obvious character in a photograph.

choices.label: Yes — small; spots above FW cell; spot at base of FW space 3; HW space 7 gap whitened
No

q_tailed_cell_silver.question: Are the cell spots on the forewing underside outlined in greenish silver?
q_tailed_cell_silver.hint: Examine the underside forewing cell. The centaurus group has a distinctive metallic greenish-silver outline around the cell spots — this is not iridescence but a true outline.

choices.label: Yes — greenish silver outlines on FW cell spots
No — no silver outlines

q_tailed_dark_area.question: Is there a distinct dark area under the forewing cell (space 1b), bounded outwardly by a pale line?
q_tailed_dark_area.hint: The camdeo group has a characteristic darkened patch in FW underside space 1b with a clearly defined pale line running across it. This feature is diagnostic for the entire camdeo group. Note: in field photographs this area is often hidden under the hindwing. If FW space 1b is not visible in your photo, select Cannot determine — the next question will let you check the camdeo subgroup characters instead.

choices.label: Yes — distinct dark patch in FW space 1b with pale bordering line
No — no such marking
Cannot determine — FW space 1b obscured by hindwing

q_camdeo_sub.question: Does this butterfly show any of the following camdeo subgroup features?
q_camdeo_sub.hint: The myrzala and bazaloides subgroups each have a distinctive hindwing underside character that is usually visible in photos. If neither applies — or if you are unsure whether this is a camdeo species at all — choose the last two options. FW spaces 1a, 1b, 2, and 3 are almost always hidden under the hindwing in perched photographs and are not used here.

choices.label: Crimson-brown underside with markings outlined in white striae; spot at base of HW space 6
Spot present mid-space 8 on the hindwing underside
Neither subgroup feature above visible — continuing within camdeo group
None of the camdeo features present — does not appear to be a camdeo species

q_ijanensis_streak.question: Is there a whitish streak on the hindwing underside running from the base of the dorsum to the apex?
q_ijanensis_streak.hint: A. ijanensis (ijanensis group) has a distinctive whitish streak on the HW underside from the base of the dorsum all the way to the apex. The HW cell is also notably long — approximately half the wing length or longer. A. anthelus and A. achelous lack this streak.

choices.label: Yes — whitish streak present on HW underside from dorsal base to apex
No — no whitish streak

q_tailed_anthelus.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 4, with a spot at the base of FW space 10 but NO spot at the base of HW space 6?
q_tailed_anthelus.hint: The anthelus group (2 spp.) is defined by partial dislocation of the FW postdiscal band at vein 4, a spot at the base of FW space 10, and the absence of a spot at the base of HW space 6. A. anthelus grahami additionally has ≥3 spots in FW space 11 and 2 subbasal spots in HW space 8; A. achelous achelous has no spots in FW space 11 and only 1 HW space 8 spot.

choices.label: Yes — FW band partially dislocated at vein 4; spot at base of FW space 10; no spot at base of HW space 6
No

q_tailed_macular.question: Are the underside markings macular — composed of separated lines and dots rather than continuous solid bands?
q_tailed_macular.hint: The democritus group has markings that are broken up into isolated dots and short lines, not forming a continuous postdiscal band. The HW postdiscal band is also widely dislocated at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — macular markings (dots/lines); HW postdiscal band widely dislocated at vein 2
No — markings form more continuous bands

q_tailed_alitaeus.question: Is there a central spot in forewing underside space 11?
q_tailed_alitaeus.hint: The alitaeus group is defined by having a central spot in FW space 11 (above the cell). Note: the anthelus group has ≥3 spots in space 11 but was already checked. The alitaeus group also has a distinctive right-angled male aedeagus (requires dissection).

choices.label: Yes — central spot in FW space 11
No

q_tailed_cleander.question: Is this a relatively large species with a conspicuously projecting tornal lobe on the hindwing, and postdiscal spots 6 and 7 placed vertically?
q_tailed_cleander.hint: The cleander group is distinguished by its large size and the prominent projecting tornal lobe (bulge at the anal angle of the hindwing). The postdiscal band is dislocated at vein 2, with spots 6 and 7 stacked vertically.

choices.label: Yes — large; conspicuous projecting HW tornal lobe; spots 6 and 7 vertical
No — or tornal lobe less conspicuous

q_cleander_sub.question: Which cleander group subgroup does the specimen belong to?
q_cleander_sub.hint: Agaba subgroup: HW postdiscal spot 5 contiguous or amalgamated with end-cell bar; HW band only PARTIALLY dislocated at vein 2 (unlike others). Cleander subgroup: large (FW > 21 mm); tornal lobe conspicuously projecting; HW band completely dislocated at v2. Alea subgroup: smaller (FW < 21 mm); tornal lobe less conspicuous.

choices.label: HW spot 5 contiguous/merged with end-cell bar; HW band partially dislocated at vein 2 (agaba subgroup)
Larger (FW > 21 mm); tornal lobe conspicuously projecting (cleander subgroup)
Smaller (FW < 21 mm); tornal lobe less conspicuous (alea subgroup)

q_tailed_agrata.question: What is the shape of the hindwing underside central cell spot, and are there specific absences on the forewing underside?
q_tailed_agrata.hint: The agrata group has two subgroups: ace (with a band-like cell spot across the whole HW cell) and agrata (lacking a FW postdiscal spot in space 9 and lacking a costal spot in space 10).

choices.label: HW central cell spot is band-like, stretching entirely across the cell (ace subgroup)
FW lacks postdiscal spot in space 9; no costal spot in FW space 10 (agrata subgroup)
Neither of these

q_tailed_spot_arrangement.question: What best describes the forewing postdiscal spot 4 and the hindwing postdiscal spot 6?
q_tailed_spot_arrangement.hint: This question separates the remaining tailed groups by the arrangement of key spots. Examine spot 4 on the FW postdiscal band and the position of spot 6 on the HW underside.

choices.label: FW spot 4 out of line with others; HW spot 6 placed directly below spot 7
FW spot 4 in continuation with spots 5 and 6; HW spot 6 rounded and more or less separated
HW spot 6 is outwardly sinuous; costal spot present in FW space 10
FW postdiscal band is continuous and unbroken; no costal spot in FW space 10; often a spot at base of FW space 3
HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 overlap with their centres forming a straight line (echelon)

q_tailed_aurea_check.question: Does the hindwing cell extend considerably longer than half the wing length, combined with an acute or concave FW apex?
q_tailed_aurea_check.hint: The aurea group (A. aurea, A. horsfieldi) has an exceptionally long hindwing cell — considerably more than half the wing length — together with an acute FW apex that may appear concave along the termen between veins 2 and 4. The eumolphus group (A. eumolphus, A. acta, etc.) has a shorter HW cell. Note that aurea males are also shining green, whereas eumolphus males are blue or purple — but use the cell length as the primary character here.

choices.label: Yes — HW cell considerably longer than half wing; FW apex acute or concave
No — HW cell moderate length (eumolphus or oenea group)

q_amphimuta_sub.question: Within the amphimuta group (echelon HW spots 5–7), which subgroup best fits the specimen?
q_amphimuta_sub.hint:
Four subgroups. Muta: spots more quadrate/banded, FW space 2 stepped toward termen. Perimuta: FW spots 2 and 3 in line (may be oblique). Kurzi: tailless, rounded HW tornus, HW band widely dislocated at vein 2. Amphimuta (largest, 12 spp.): spots rounded, cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

If FW spots 2–3 alignment is unclear, choose 'Cannot determine (spots 2–3)' — the perimuta species can still be separated by their own distinctive characters (yellowish HW area for perimuta; contrasting HW colour for metamuta; spot 6 below spot 7 + thread border for antimuta).

choices.label: Spots more quadrate/banded; FW space 2 spot stepped outward toward termen (muta subgroup)
FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup)
Tailless; rounded HW tornus; HW postdiscal band widely dislocated at vein 2 (kurzi subgroup)
Spots rounded; cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2 (amphimuta subgroup)
Cannot determine — FW spots 2 and 3 alignment not visible (check perimuta species characters)

q_tailless_fulla.question: Does the forewing underside completely lack an end-cell bar, and are the markings generally obscure or faint?
q_tailless_fulla.hint: The fulla group is unusual in lacking the end-cell bar on the FW underside. The overall underside markings are usually indistinct or obscure.

choices.label: Yes — no end-cell bar on FW underside; markings obscure
No — end-cell bar present or markings clear

q_tailless_inornata.question: Does the hindwing have a very rounded tornus, and are tornal green scales absent?
q_tailless_inornata.hint: The inornata group has a distinctively rounded hindwing tornus (anal angle). Unlike many other tailless Arhopala, it lacks metallic green or brassy tornal scales.

choices.label: Yes — very rounded HW tornus; no tornal green scales
No

q_tailless_hypomuta.question: Is there a small spot at the extreme base of hindwing space 6 on the underside?
q_tailless_hypomuta.hint: This spot sits right at the base of space 6, closer to the wing base than the normal postdiscal spot row. It distinguishes the hypomuta group from the similar amphimuta group in most Malayan specimens. Note: the spot is usually absent in Bornean and Sumatran material.

choices.label: Yes — small spot at extreme base of HW space 6
No

q_tailless_agesias.question: Are the underside spots prominent and rounded, with a central spot in FW space 11 and a notably short forewing vein 9?
q_tailless_agesias.hint: The agesias group has large, rounded, prominent spots (not narrow bands). Key features: central spot in FW space 11 (above the cell), and FW vein 9 is noticeably shorter than usual.

choices.label: Yes — prominent rounded spots; central FW spot in space 11; FW vein 9 notably short
No

q_tailless_epimuta.question: Do the underside forewing markings blend into the ground colour with very low contrast — nearly invisible against the background?
q_tailless_epimuta.hint: The epimuta group is unique among Arhopala in having underside markings that are remarkably faint — the lines and spots barely show against the ground colour. In non-epimuta species the underside markings form clearly-defined lines or spots with moderate to strong contrast. If the underside is not visible, choose 'Cannot determine' — the next step will check the male forewing upperside instead.

choices.label: Yes — underside markings blend in, very low contrast
No — underside markings clearly defined with moderate to strong contrast
Cannot determine — underside not visible

q_tailless_epimuta_upper.question: Does the male forewing upperside have a distinctly differently-coloured or more opaque discal patch?
q_tailless_epimuta_upper.hint: The epimuta group males have a forewing discal patch that is noticeably more opaque or differently textured than the surrounding shining blue upperside. If neither underside nor upperside is assessable, choose 'Cannot determine' to proceed past this check.

choices.label: Yes — male FW upperside has opaque or differently-coloured discal patch
No
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_tailless_agesilaus.question: On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal spot 6 placed equidistantly between spot 5 and the end-cell bar?
q_tailless_agesilaus.hint: The agesilaus group can be confused with the amphimuta group. Its defining feature is HW spot 6 placed equidistant from spot 5 and the end-cell bar — not closer to one or the other. Tailless.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 equidistant between spot 5 and end-cell bar
No

q_tailless_agelastus_check.question: Is the forewing underside postdiscal band continuous and unbroken, often with a small spot at the base of space 3?
q_tailless_agelastus_check.hint: The agelastus group has a continuous (non-dislocated) FW postdiscal band, often with a spot at the base of FW space 3. Most species lack a costal spot in FW space 10, though one species (epimete suedas) is an exception.

choices.label: Yes — FW postdiscal band continuous; often a spot at base of FW space 3
No

q_tailless_camdeo.question: Is there a distinct dark area under the forewing cell (space 1b), bounded outwardly by a pale line?
q_tailless_camdeo.hint: A small number of camdeo group species are tailless (A. belphoebe cowani, A. myrzalina). The camdeo group is identified by a darkened patch in FW underside space 1b. In field photos this area is often hidden under the hindwing — if so, select Cannot determine and check the camdeo subgroup characters instead.

choices.label: Yes — distinct dark patch in FW space 1b with pale bordering line
No — no such marking
Cannot determine — FW space 1b obscured by hindwing

q_tailless_echelon.question: Do hindwing postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 overlap with their centres forming a straight line (echelon arrangement)?
q_tailless_echelon.hint: The amphimuta group (muta, perimuta, kurzi, and amphimuta subgroups) is defined by HW spots 5, 6, and 7 having their centres in a straight line — an 'echelon'. Most amphimuta-group species are tailless. If present, this character routes to a subgroup choice.

choices.label: Yes — HW spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
No — spots not in echelon

q_tailless_rama.question: Is hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly sinuous, and is there a costal spot present in forewing underside space 1?
q_tailless_rama.hint: Arhopala arvina adalitas is the only tailless species in the rama group. Its key underside characters are an S-shaped (outwardly sinuous) HW postdiscal spot 6 and a costal spot at the base of FW space 1. Underside ground colour is hair-brown; tornal green scales are present.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 outwardly sinuous; costal spot in FW space 1
No
Cannot determine

g_centaurus.description: Separated from all other groups by having the FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver on the underside. HW cell short — significantly less than half the wing length. Two Malayan species (centaurus nakula, araxes) plus A. amantes (South Myanmar) which is keyed here due to similar silvery markings but is technically outside the group.
key_features: FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver (underside)
HW cell short: noticeably less than half wing length
Tailed

g_anthelus.description: Forewing postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 4. Spot present at base of FW space 10; NO spot at base of HW space 6. Two Malayan species: A. anthelus grahami (large, 28 mm; extra spots in FW space 11 and HW space 8) and A. achelous achelous (smaller, 22 mm; no extra spots).
key_features: FW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 4
Spot at base of FW space 10
No spot at base of HW space 6

g_camdeo_general.description: Defined by a dark area under the forewing cell bounded outwardly by a pale line across space 1b, with HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, and 5 arranged in echelon. No myrzala or bazaloides subgroup character is visible. Most species here belong to the camdeo subgroup (9 species, the largest subgroup), which often features a small spot at the extreme base of FW space 3 — though this character is usually hidden under the hindwing. Continue below to key out the individual species.
key_features: Dark area under FW cell (space 1b) bounded by pale line
HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, 5 in echelon
Small spot sometimes at extreme base of FW space 3 (camdeo subgroup; often obscured)

g_camdeo_camdeo.description: The largest camdeo subgroup (9 species). Often features a small spot at the extreme base of FW space 3. Includes both tailed and tailless species and a wide range of upperside colours from pale silvery-blue to deep bluish-purple.
key_features: Dark area under FW cell (space 1b) bounded by pale line
Small spot often at extreme base of FW space 3
HW postdiscal spots 7, 6, 5 in echelon

g_camdeo_myrzala.description: Easily recognized by the crimson-brown underside with markings outlined by prominent white striae, and a spot always present at the base of HW space 6. Two species separated by tail presence and border width.
key_features: Crimson-brown underside with markings outlined by white striae
Spot at base of HW space 6
Dark area under FW cell (space 1b)

g_camdeo_bazaloides.description: Unique within the camdeo group for having an extra spot in mid-space 8 on the HW underside. Underside is purple-brown with white striae but LACKS the HW space 6 basal spot that defines the myrzala subgroup. One species.
key_features: Extra spot mid-space 8 on HW underside
Purple-brown underside with white striae
No spot at base of HW space 6 (unlike myrzala subgroup)
Dark area under FW cell (space 1b)

g_agesias.description: Tailless, with slightly dentate (toothed) HW margins. Both sexes similar: shining, powdery blue with broad black borders (~1.5 mm at tornus, expanding to mid-costa). Prominent rounded spots on the underside; at least one subcostal spot in FW space 11. FW vein 9 notably short. Three Malaysian species.
key_features: Tailless (HW margins slightly dentate)
Sexes alike: shining powdery blue; broad black borders
At least one subcostal spot above FW cell in space 11
FW vein 9 notably short

g_ijanensis.description: Tailed species with an unusually long hindwing cell (~half wing length or longer). Underside: spot in subcostal mid-space 11 above the central cell spot; no spot at base of FW space 10; spot always at base of HW space 6. The male FW border runs along the costa to the wing base.
key_features: Tailed
HW cell ~half wing length or longer
FW underside: subcostal spot in space 11; no spot at base of space 10
Spot at base of HW space 6
Male: FW costal border runs to wing base

g_epimuta.description: Males are instantly recognizable by a discal patch on the forewing upperside that is differently coloured and more opaque than the surrounding blue (dense fuscous scales underlying the blue). On the underside, HW postdiscal spot 6 is placed midway between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. Two species are tailed; one is tailless.
key_features: Male: opaque or differently-coloured discal patch on FW upperside
HW postdiscal spot 6 midway between spot 5 and end-cell bar
Two tailed spp. (atosia, lurida); one tailless sp. (epimuta)

g_agesilaus.description: Tailless species that can be confused with the amphimuta group. Defined by HW postdiscal spot 6 placed equidistantly between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Two Malaysian species.
key_features: Tailless
HW postdiscal spot 6 equidistant between spot 5 and end-cell bar
HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2

q_agesilaus_s1.question: Is a basal cell spot present on the forewing underside?
q_agesilaus_s1.hint: Arhopala agesilaus gesa has a basal cell spot on the FW underside (typical for the genus). Arhopala avatha uniquely lacks this basal cell spot — its absence is diagnostic.

choices.label: Yes — basal cell spot present on FW underside
No — basal cell spot absent (unique within this group)

r_agesilaus_gesa.name: Arhopala agesilaus gesa
r_agesilaus_gesa.note: Tailless. FW underside has a basal cell spot. Male: rich violet blue; FW black border 2.0 mm at tornus expanding to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: bluer shade than male. Superficially resembles a small Arhopala major.

r_avatha.name: Arhopala avatha
r_avatha.note: Tailless. Uniquely lacks the basal cell spot on FW underside. Male: very dark bluish violet; FW black border 1.0 mm at tornus increasing to 2.0 mm at apex (narrow borders). Female: purple. Similar to Arhopala antimuta but distinguished by wider borders in the male.

g_oenea.description: FW apex acute and termen straight between veins 2–4. Underside postdiscal spots narrow, regular, and rectangular; FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4. Monotypic in the Peninsula. ⚠ Note: the Malayan representative (allata pandora) is tailed — routing via the tailless chain may need review.
key_features: FW apex acute; termen straight between veins 2–4
Underside postdiscal spots narrow, regular, rectangular
FW band sinuous or slightly dislocated at vein 4
Tailed (allata pandora)

g_amphimuta_muta.description: Postdiscal spots are more quadrate (squarish/banded). The FW postdiscal spot in space 2 is stepped outward toward the termen, offset from the main band. Three Malaysian species.
key_features: HW spots 5, 6, 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
Spots more quadrate/banded in shape
FW space 2 spot stepped outward toward termen

g_amphimuta_perimuta.description: The FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 are in line with each other. Two Malaysian species, one easily recognized by a yellowish discal area on the HW underside.
key_features: HW spots 5, 6, 7 in echelon (centres in straight line)
FW postdiscal spots in spaces 2 and 3 are in line

g_hypomuta.description: Tailless species, often confused with the amphimuta group. Most Malayan individuals are distinguished by a small spot at the extreme base of HW space 6 on the underside (this spot is usually absent in Sumatran and Bornean specimens). Monotypic in the Malay Peninsula.
key_features: Tailless
Usually: small spot at extreme base of HW space 6 (Malayan ssp.)
FW ~19 mm

g_inornata.description: Tailless with a very rounded hindwing tornus. On the underside: no tornal green scales; HW postdiscal spot 6 placed immediately below spot 7. Monotypic.
key_features: Tailless; very rounded HW tornus
No tornal green scales on underside
HW spot 6 placed immediately below spot 7
FW ~21 mm

g_democritus.description: Underside markings macular — separated lines and dots. Short HW tail 1.5–2.0 mm. Costal spot in mid-FW space 11; FW spot 4 shifted distad; HW spot 6 outwardly concave; HW band widely dislocated at vein 2. Two Malayan subspecies of A. democritus separated by underside colour.
key_features: Macular underside (separated dots and lines)
Short HW tail 1.5–2.0 mm
Costal spot in mid-FW space 11; FW spot 4 shifted distad
HW spot 6 outwardly concave; HW band widely dislocated at vein 2

g_alitaeus.description: Defined by having a central spot in FW space 11 on the underside and HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex. Tailed species (tail 3.5–4.0 mm, except A. denta ~2.5 mm). Nine Malaysian species.
key_features: Central spot in FW underside space 11
HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex
Tailed (3.5–4.0 mm; A. denta only ~2.5 mm)
Male aedeagus right-angled before apex (dissection required)

g_agrata_ace.description: Recognizable by the hindwing underside central cell spot, which is band-like and stretches entirely across the cell. Two Malaysian species, separated by the FW postdiscal band dislocation and the shape of HW postdiscal spot 6.
key_features: HW underside central cell spot band-like, stretching entirely across the cell

q_agrata_ace_s1.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band clearly dislocated at vein 4, and is hindwing postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave?
q_agrata_ace_s1.hint: Arhopala ace ace has the FW band distinctly dislocated at vein 4 with HW spot 6 outwardly concave; all markings are relatively wide and the male is deep cobalt blue with a thread-like border. Arhopala azinis azinis has the FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4, HW spot 6 outwardly straight or convex, and the male has broad borders; it is strictly montane.

choices.label: Yes — FW band clearly dislocated at vein 4; HW spot 6 outwardly concave
No — FW band sinuous or only slightly dislocated; HW spot 6 straight or convex

r_ace_ace.name: Arhopala ace ace
r_ace_ace.note: Underside: all markings relatively wide; FW postdiscal band dislocated at vein 4; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave. Male: deep cobalt blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female: purple.

r_azinis_azinis.name: Arhopala azinis azinis
r_azinis_azinis.note: Underside: FW postdiscal band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly straight or convex. Male: dark blue with broad black borders (2.0 mm at FW mid-termen, 3.0 mm on HW). Female: purple. Strictly montane habitat.

g_agrata_agrata.description: Identified by specific absences on the forewing underside: the postdiscal spot in space 9 is absent or very obscure, and there is never a costal spot in space 10. One Malaysian species.
key_features: FW underside: postdiscal spot in space 9 absent or very obscure
FW underside: no costal spot in space 10
HW postdiscal spot 6 considerably wider than spot 7
All underside markings tend to be rather faint

r_agrata_agrata.name: Arhopala agrata agrata
r_agrata_agrata.note: Underside markings faint. FW: postdiscal spot in space 9 absent or very obscure; no costal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal spot 6 considerably wider than spot 7. Male: dark purplish blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female: bright blue to purple-blue; HW blue reaches the termen between vein endings.

g_cleander.description: Large species (FW > 21 mm) with a conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe. HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. Four species separated by FW band dislocation at vein 4 and by HW spot 6 position relative to spots 5 and the end-cell bar.
key_features: FW > 21 mm; conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe
HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2
HW spots 6 and 7 placed one below the other

g_cleander_alea.description: Smaller species (FW < 21 mm) within the cleander group with a less conspicuous HW tornal lobe. Nine species with diverse underside colours from slatey purple to slatey pink; genitalia often needed for certain identification.
key_features: FW < 21 mm; HW tornal lobe less conspicuous
HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2
Male genitalia often required for certain ID

q_tailed_spot4_outofline.question: Are the underside postdiscal spots uniformly narrow and rectangular throughout the band, combined with an acute FW apex and nearly straight termen (between veins 2–4)?
q_tailed_spot4_outofline.hint: This question separates the oenea group (A. allata pandora — monotypic on the Peninsula) from the eumolphus group. The oenea group has a COMBINATION of (1) underside postdiscal spots that are narrow, elongate, and rectangular throughout the band, and (2) an acute FW apex with termen nearly straight between veins 2 and 4. Important: the eumolphus group contains BOTH rounded-apex species (e.g. A. eumolphus, A. acta) AND acute-apex species (e.g. A. tameanga, A. hellenore) — so apex shape alone is not sufficient. If the underside spots are not uniformly narrow and rectangular, answer 'No' regardless of apex shape.

choices.label: Yes — underside spots uniformly narrow and rectangular; FW apex acute, termen nearly straight
No — underside spots not uniformly narrow rectangular (eumolphus group, rounded or acute apex)

g_eumolphus.description: Tailed species where the forewing postdiscal spot 4 is moved out of line with the others. On the hindwing, postdiscal spot 6 is placed directly below spot 7. This group includes species with shining green males.
key_features: Tailed
FW postdiscal spot 4 out of line with the other spots
HW postdiscal spot 6 placed directly below spot 7
Some males: shining green upperside

g_vihara.description: Tailed. FW 20–22 mm. Distinguished from the similar eumolphus group by FW postdiscal spot 4 being in line with spots 5 and 6 (not moved out of line). Also lacks a costal spot in FW space 10. HW postdiscal spot 6 rounded, more or less separated. HW band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Monotypic.
key_features: Tailed; FW 20–22 mm
FW spot 4 in line with spots 5–6 (cf. eumolphus: spot 4 out of line)
No costal spot in FW space 10
HW spot 6 rounded, more or less separated
HW band partially dislocated at vein 2

g_rama.description: HW postdiscal spot 6 is outwardly sinuous or concave. A costal spot is normally present in FW space 10 on the underside. Three rare Malayan species: one tailless (arvina), two tailed (paralea 2.5 mm, buddha 1.0 mm).
key_features: HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly sinuous or concave
Costal spot normally present in FW space 10
Three spp.: one tailless, two tailed (different tail lengths)

g_agelastus.description: FW underside postdiscal band is continuous; often a spot at base of FW space 3. Most species lack a costal spot in FW space 10 (exception: epimete suedas). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Most species are tailless; males typically have broad black borders. Seven Malaysian species in four subgroups.
key_features: FW postdiscal band continuous; often spot at base of FW space 3
HW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 2
Most species tailless (barami penanga is the exception)
Males: broad black borders, often similar to females

g_corinda.description: Monotypic. Filamentous HW tail ~3.5 mm. FW: pointed apex, straight termen. FW underside: basal half of space 1b completely darkened (large purple-brown patch); spot 4 shifted distad; no costal spot in FW space 10. HW underside: spot 6 outwardly convex; band partially dislocated at vein 2. Male deep violet/dark purple, very narrow border.
key_features: Filamentous HW tail ~3.5 mm
FW underside: large purple-brown patch in basal half of space 1b
FW spot 4 shifted distad; no costal spot in space 10
HW spot 6 outwardly convex; band partially dislocated at vein 2

g_aurea.description: Males have a HW cell considerably longer than half the wing length, a rather acute FW apex, and a straight or slightly concave termen. Males are shining green above; females are blue or purple-blue. All members possess a short stumpy HW tail (just under 2.0 mm). Five species (4 Peninsular, 1 Bornean).
key_features: Male: HW cell considerably longer than half wing length
Male: FW apex acute; termen straight or slightly concave
Male upperside: shining green; female blue or purple-blue
Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm)

g_ganesa.description: Small tailed species. Underside FW has a characteristic spot at the base of space 11, positioned between the basal and central cell spots, plus a spot at the base of space 3. On the HW underside, the gap between the outer and central spots in space 7 is conspicuously whitened — a shared group character visible across all five Peninsular species. Keyed within the group by tornal green scales and HW spot 6 length.
key_features: Small; short HW tail
FW underside: spot at base of space 11 (between basal and central cell spots)
FW underside: spot at base of space 3
HW underside: gap between outer and central spots in space 7 conspicuously whitened

g_fulla.description: Tailless species lacking an end-cell bar on the forewing underside. Underside markings are usually obscure or very faint. Monotypic in Malaysia.
key_features: Tailless
FW underside: no end-cell bar
Underside markings usually obscure
Only one Malaysian species: Arhopala fulla intaca

g_abseus.description: Features a short white-tipped tail at vein 3 of the hindwing (not the usual vein 2), and a spot at the base of hindwing space 1a. Forewing vein 9 is very short, arising well beyond the end of vein 10. Two species in Malaysia.
key_features: Short white-tipped tail at HW vein 3
Spot at base of HW space 1a
FW vein 9 very short (arises well beyond end of vein 10)

q_abseus_species.question: What is the underside ground colour and are the markings darkened?
q_abseus_species.hint: Three species share the vein 3 white-tipped tail. abseus has strongly darkened (blackish-brown) underside markings and a costal spot in FW space 12. anella is larger with hair-brown underside and no spot in FW space 12. alesia sacharja (recorded in Langkawi; also NE India to Vietnam) is a late Malayan record — detailed underside characters for separation from anella are not fully documented; use distribution as a supplementary guide.

choices.label: Markings much darkened (blackish brown); costal spot present in FW space 12
Underside hair brown; no spot in FW space 12; male with very wide fuscous borders on upperside
Langkawi (or NE India / Myanmar / Thailand / Vietnam range)

r_abseus_abseus.name: Arhopala abseus
r_abseus_abseus.note: Underside markings much darkened — most spots blackish brown. Forewing underside: costal spot in space 12; two spots each in spaces 9, 10, and 11. Hindwing: spot in middle of space 7 outlined in whitish. Male upperside: deep lustrous violet blue; FW black border expands from 2.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: shining blue with wider borders.

r_anella.name: Arhopala anella
r_anella.note: Considerably larger than A. abseus. Underside ground colour hair brown; markings not prominently darkened; no spot in FW space 12. Male upperside: blue restricted to basal halves of FW spaces 1b, 2, and 3 and the lower half of the cell; HW blue confined almost entirely to the cell; very wide fuscous borders. Female: relatively narrow black borders.

r_alesia_sacharja.name: Arhopala alesia sacharja
r_alesia_sacharja.note: Short white-tipped tail at vein 3 (abseus group). First recorded in Malaysia from Langkawi in 1994. Also distributed across NE India, Myanmar, Thailand, Laos, and Vietnam. ⚠ Detailed underside characters to separate from A. anella are not fully documented in Malayan material — distribution is currently the most reliable field indicator for this subspecies.

g_aedias.description: Characterised by a very long filamentous tail at vein 2 (~6.0 mm). HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 are in echelon. No dark area under the FW cell, and no spot at the base of HW space 6. One Malaysian species.
key_features: Long filamentous tail at vein 2 (~6.0 mm)
HW postdiscal spots 5, 6, and 7 in echelon
No dark area under the FW cell
No spot at base of HW space 6

r_aedias.name: Arhopala aedias
r_aedias.note: Underside ground colour hair brown; spots narrowly outlined in pale buff lines. Male upperside: lustrous violet blue with black border reduced to a thread. Female upperside: purple blue with typical broad black borders. Two size forms occur: large (FW 25–29 mm) and small (FW 20–22 mm; small form has more rounded, darker spots beneath). Two subspecies: A. aedias agnis (forest at low to moderate elevations, standard) and A. aedias meritatas (Langkawi Islands — paler and brighter blue upperside in both sexes).

features.What is the underside ground colour and: Hair brown underside; spots narrowly buff-outlined; large species (FW 20–29 mm); rounded FW apex — A. aedias

q_agelastus_s1.question: Is there a costal spot in forewing space 10?
q_agelastus_s1.hint: A costal spot in FW space 10 is unique to Arhopala epimete suedas within this group. All other agelastus group species lack it.

choices.label: Yes — costal spot present in FW space 10
No — FW space 10 lacks a costal spot

q_agelastus_s2.question: Within the agelastus group, does the hindwing have a tail?
q_agelastus_s2.hint: Within the agelastus group, only Arhopala barami penanga is tailed. All other species are tailless.

choices.label: Yes — hindwing is tailed
No — hindwing is tailless

q_agelastus_s3.question: Are the underside markings small and macular (dot-like), and are tornal green scales absent from the hindwing?
q_agelastus_s3.hint: Arhopala cardoni has characteristically small, macular (isolated dot-like) markings on the underside. The hindwing also lacks the metallic tornal green scales seen in some other species. The HW is evenly rounded.

choices.label: Yes — small macular markings; no tornal green scales; HW evenly rounded
No — markings larger or not macular

q_agelastus_s4.question: Are the inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2–3 and HW spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened, and is HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous?
q_agelastus_s4.hint: Arhopala alaconia media (alesia subgroup) is distinguished by strikingly enlarged and darkened inner submarginal spots in these specific spaces, combined with an outwardly concave or sinuous HW postdiscal spot 6.

choices.label: Yes — inner submarginal spots enlarged and darkened; HW spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous
No

q_agelastus_s5.question: Is the male upperside notably darker violet with narrower black borders than typical for the group?
q_agelastus_s5.hint: A. labuana is a Bornean endemic with a notably darker violet male and narrower black borders than other members of this group. If the male upperside is not visible, choose 'Cannot determine' to continue — but note that if the specimen is from Borneo, A. labuana should be considered.

choices.label: Yes — much darker violet; borders notably narrower
No — violet blue
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_agelastus_s6.question: How broad is the male forewing black border?
q_agelastus_s6.hint: Wildeyana has broader borders (2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa) than agelastus (1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex). Females: wildeyana is brighter/purer blue; agelastus female is brighter blue with broader borders; in agelastus, the underside HW space 3 spot is nearly in line with spots 2 and 4.

choices.label: Broad: 2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa
Standard: 1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex

r_epimete_suedas.name: Arhopala epimete suedas
r_epimete_suedas.note: Unique in the agelastus group: costal spot present in FW space 10. FW underside: lower portion of postdiscal band significantly wider than upper part. HW lacks tornal green scales. Both sexes alike: blue with broad FW borders (2.0 mm at tornus to 6.0 mm at costa). Blue on HW confined to the cell. Tailless.

r_barami_penanga.name: Arhopala barami penanga
r_barami_penanga.note: The only tailed species in the agelastus group. Male upperside: blue with a broad FW border expanding from 2.5 mm at the tornus to 8.0 mm at the costa. Female: paler blue.

r_cardoni.name: Arhopala cardoni
r_cardoni.note: Tailless; evenly rounded HW tornus with no tooth or tail at vein 2. Underside markings small and macular (dot-like); no tornal green scales. Male: bright blue with violet tinge; FW border 2.0 mm at tornus expanding to 5.0 mm at apex. Female: purer blue with slightly wider black borders than the male.

r_alaconia_media.name: Arhopala alaconia media
r_alaconia_media.note: Alesia subgroup. Underside: inner submarginal spots in FW spaces 2–3 and HW spaces 4–5 notably enlarged and darkened; HW postdiscal spot 6 outwardly concave or sinuous. Normally tailless (slightly toothed), but a rare tailed form (f. kempi) exists. Both sexes alike: violet blue with FW border 3.0 mm at tornus expanding to mid-costa.

r_labuana.name: Arhopala labuana
r_labuana.note: Male upperside: much darker violet than A. agelastus, with narrower black borders. Female: dark blue. Tailless.

r_wildeyana.name: Arhopala wildeyana wildeyana
r_wildeyana.note: Male upperside: violet blue with broader borders than A. labuana or A. agelastus — 2.0 mm at tornus to 5.0 mm at costa. Female: brighter and purer blue. Tailless.

r_agelastus_agelastus.name: Arhopala agelastus agelastus
r_agelastus_agelastus.note: Underside: HW postdiscal spot in space 3 is almost in line with spots in spaces 2 and 4 (distinguishes female from allied species). Male: violet blue with FW border 1.0 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: brighter blue with broader borders. Tailless.

q_agesias_s1.question: How many spots are in forewing space 11, and what is the underside ground colour?
q_agesias_s1.hint: Arhopala kinabala is distinguished by having TWO spots in FW space 11, a dark brown ground colour, and blackish-brown markings. A. agesias and A. similis have a single spot in space 11 and a hair-brown ground colour.

choices.label: Two spots in FW space 11; ground colour dark brown; markings blackish-brown
Single spot in FW space 11; ground colour hair brown

q_agesias_s2.question: Is the postdiscal band completely absent from the forewing underside?
q_agesias_s2.hint: Arhopala similis is superficially identical to A. agesias but uniquely lacks the postdiscal band entirely on the FW underside. A. agesias has the normal rounded-spot postdiscal band present.

choices.label: Yes — postdiscal band completely absent from FW underside
No — postdiscal band present (rounded spots)

r_kinabala.name: Arhopala kinabala
r_kinabala.note: Tailless. Underside ground colour dark brown with large, blackish-brown markings. FW space 11 has two spots; FW space 3 spot is elongate and touches the end-cell bar. HW postdiscal spot 6 is oval, significantly enlarged, and overlaps the end-cell bar. Rare; seldom found below 450 m elevation.

r_similis.name: Arhopala similis
r_similis.note: Tailless. Superficially identical to A. agesias but uniquely lacks the postdiscal band on the FW underside. Status as a separate species (vs. dimorph of A. agesias) has been questioned, though female genitalia differ slightly.

r_agesias.name: Arhopala agesias
r_agesias.note: Tailless. The most common species in the group. Underside: hair-brown ground colour with all markings as rounded spots; single central spot in FW space 11.

q_alitaeus_s1.question: Is there a spot at the extreme base of forewing underside space 10 (just above vein 10, near the cell-end)?
q_alitaeus_s1.hint: Look for a small isolated spot right at the base of FW space 10, distinct from the regular postdiscal row. Species with this spot: pseudomuta, sintanga, alitaeus, denta. Species without: elopura, arianaga, ariana, aida, havilandi.

choices.label: Yes — spot present at extreme base of FW space 10
No — no spot at extreme base of FW space 10

q_alitaeus_s1_tail.question: Is the hindwing tail notably short, approximately 2.5 mm (rather than the typical 3.5–4.0 mm for the group)?
q_alitaeus_s1_tail.hint: A. denta is the only alitaeus-group member with a distinctly shortened tail (~2.5 mm). All others have tails of 3.5–4.0 mm.

choices.label: Yes — tail short, ~2.5 mm
No — tail of normal length, 3.5–4.0 mm

q_alitaeus_s1_color.question: Is the underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings?
q_alitaeus_s1_color.hint: A. sintanga has a distinctly pale slatey-purple ground colour with markings that strongly contrast in reddish-brown. A. pseudomuta and A. alitaeus are hair-brown underneath.

choices.label: Yes — pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings
No — hair-brown or dark brownish underside

q_alitaeus_s1_marks.question: Are the underside markings notably annular (ring-like) and is the FW postdiscal spot in space 1b often dislocated outward relative to the spot in space 2?
q_alitaeus_s1_marks.hint: A. alitaeus has more annular (ring-like) markings than allied species, and the space 1b spot is often shifted outward; male has regular black borders at least 1.0 mm wide. A. pseudomuta has markings that are hardly darker than the hair-brown ground colour; HW space 6 is slightly oblique and does not overlap the end-cell bar.

choices.label: Yes — annular markings; FW space 1b spot often dislocated outward; borders ≥1.0 mm
No — markings barely darker than ground colour; HW space 6 slightly oblique

q_alitaeus_s2_color.question: Is the underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings?
q_alitaeus_s2_color.hint: A. havilandi has a pale slatey-purple ground colour with strongly contrasting reddish-brown markings and a thread-like black border on the male upperside. If the underside is hair-brown or dark brownish, choose No.

choices.label: Yes — pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings
No — hair-brown or dark brownish underside

q_alitaeus_s2_glaze.question: Is the underside dark brownish (not hair-brown) with a distinct slatey or purplish glaze?
q_alitaeus_s2_glaze.hint: A. ariana is dark brown with a faint purplish wash; A. aida is brown with a slatey to purplish glaze and a slightly produced FW apex. A. elopura and A. arianaga are hair-brown with markings barely darker than the ground colour.

choices.label: Yes — dark brownish with a slatey or purplish glaze
No — hair-brown, markings barely darker than ground colour

q_alitaeus_s2_term.question: Is the forewing termen notably straight and the apex slightly produced (pointed)?
q_alitaeus_s2_term.hint: A. aida has a distinctly straight FW termen with a slightly produced apex, and the male black border expands from ~1.25 mm at the tornus to ~2.0 mm at the apex. A. ariana has a more normal wing shape; FW postdiscal spot 4 is shifted distad out of line with adjacent spots.

choices.label: Yes — FW termen straight; apex slightly produced; border widens toward apex
No — FW shape normal; postdiscal spot 4 shifted distad; border 1.0–1.5 mm

q_alitaeus_s2_sp6.question: Is the hindwing postdiscal space 6 partly fuscous (darkened)?
q_alitaeus_s2_sp6.hint: A. elopura has HW space 6 partly fuscous (dark). In A. arianaga, HW space 6 is entirely blue; underside markings are slightly darker than the ground colour with a faint pinkish glaze, and the male border is reduced to a thread.

choices.label: Yes — HW space 6 partly fuscous; male upperside purer blue, border ~0.5 mm
No — HW space 6 entirely blue; faint pinkish glaze; male border thread-like

r_pseudomuta.name: Arhopala pseudomuta
r_pseudomuta.note: Underside hair-brown; markings hardly darker than ground colour. FW: basal spot present in space 10. HW: postdiscal space 6 slightly oblique, does not overlap end-cell bar. Male: violet-blue with black border ~1.0 mm. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_elopura.name: Arhopala elopura
r_elopura.note: Underside hair-brown; markings barely darker than ground colour — contrast less than in A. pseudomuta. FW: no basal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal space 6 partly fuscous. Male: purer blue with black border ~0.5 mm. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_arianaga.name: Arhopala arianaga
r_arianaga.note: Underside markings slightly darker than ground colour with a very faint pinkish glaze. FW: no basal spot in space 10. HW: postdiscal space 6 entirely blue. Male: black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_ariana.name: Arhopala ariana
r_ariana.note: Underside dark brown with a faint purplish wash. FW: no basal spot in space 10; postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad out of line. Male: border 1.0–1.5 mm, slightly wider at apex. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_aida.name: Arhopala aida
r_aida.note: Underside brown with a slatey to purplish glaze. FW: no basal spot in space 10; termen straight, apex slightly produced. Male: border expands from ~1.25 mm at tornus to ~2.0 mm at apex. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_havilandi.name: Arhopala havilandi
r_havilandi.note: Underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings. FW: no basal spot in space 10. Male: violet-blue, black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_sintanga.name: Arhopala sintanga
r_sintanga.note: Underside pale slatey purple with contrasting reddish-brown markings. FW: basal spot present in space 10. Male: dark violet, black border reduced to a thread. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_alitaeus.name: Arhopala alitaeus
r_alitaeus.note: Underside markings notably annular (ring-like); FW postdiscal spot in space 1b often dislocated outward from spot in space 2. FW: basal spot present in space 10. Male: regular black borders ≥1.0 mm wide. Tail ~3.5–4.0 mm.

r_denta.name: Arhopala denta
r_denta.note: Distinctive SHORT tail — only ~2.5 mm (others in the group have 3.5–4.0 mm). FW: basal spot present in space 10. Underside hair-brown with at most a slight pinkish glaze. Male: brilliant blue, black border reduced to a thread.

r_kurzi.name: Arhopala kurzi
r_kurzi.note: Tailless; HW tornus very rounded. FW 18–22 mm. Male: rather dull purple-blue with a thread-like black border. Female: purple; HW border unusually variable — purple sometimes reaches termen between veins 2 and 5, other specimens almost confined to cell. Underside FW: markings variable; lower postdiscal band sometimes obsolete; when fully developed, spot in space 1b shifted inward, placed midway between end-cell bar and postdiscal spot in space 2. Underside HW: postdiscal band WIDELY dislocated at vein 2 (diagnostic for subgroup). Forest species, low to moderate elevations.

features.On the forewing underside, is the basal: No — basal cell spot present on FW underside
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: No — no white patch on HW cilia

q_amph_s1.question: Does the hindwing have a short tail (tooth-like projection) at vein 2?
q_amph_s1.hint: A. delta is the ONLY tailed member of the amphimuta subgroup. All other amphimuta subgroup species are tailless.

choices.label: Yes — tailed at vein 2
No — tailless

q_amph_s2.question: On the forewing underside, is the basal cell spot absent?
q_amph_s2.hint: A. avathina avathina is recognized by the absence of the basal cell spot on the FW underside; additionally, the HW postdiscal band is completely dislocated at vein 2. All other amphimuta subgroup members have a basal cell spot.

choices.label: Yes — no basal cell spot on FW underside (HW band also completely dislocated at vein 2)
No — basal cell spot present on FW underside

q_amph_s3.question: Is there a distinct white patch on the hindwing cilia just above the tornal lobe?
q_amph_s3.hint: A. norda has a small but distinctive white patch on the HW cilia just above the tornal lobe, and also has less rounded (more angular) postdiscal spots compared to A. major.

choices.label: Yes — white patch on HW cilia above tornal lobe; postdiscal spots less rounded
No — no white patch on HW cilia

q_amph_s4.question: Is the male's black border broad, 1.5 mm or wider?
q_amph_s4.hint: A. major has a border expanding from 2.0 mm at tornus to 3.0 mm at apex (blue upperside). A. stubbsi has a broad ~2.0 mm border but is dull bluish purple. All others have narrow borders (0.5 mm or thread-like). If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — the key will continue using underside characters.

choices.label: Yes — border ≥1.5 mm wide
No — border narrow (0.5 mm or thread-like)
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_broad.question: Is the male distinctly blue above, with the border widening from ~2.0 mm at the tornus to ~3.0 mm at the apex?
q_amph_broad.hint: A. major major is bluer than A. amphimuta; its border expands toward the apex. A. stubbsi is dull bluish purple with a broad but even ~2.0 mm border. If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — A. major and A. stubbsi cannot be reliably separated from the underside alone.

choices.label: Yes — distinctly blue; border expands 2.0→3.0 mm from tornus to apex
No — dull bluish purple; border broad but even (~2.0 mm)
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_s5.question: Is the male's black border reduced to a thread (very narrow, well under 0.5 mm)?
q_amph_s5.hint: Peninsula thread-border species: A. moolaiana yajuna (shining violet-blue; female blue reaches HW termen). Bornean thread-border species (A. alica) have already been separated by the geography filter. Regular 0.5 mm border Peninsula species: A. amphimuta, A. sceva, A. zylda. If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — the key will continue using underside characters.

choices.label: Yes — border thread-like
No — border regular ~0.5 mm
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_s6.question: Is the hindwing underside postdiscal series dislocated at vein 2, and is the species noticeably smaller than its allies?
q_amph_s6.hint: A. sceva indra has the HW postdiscal series clearly dislocated at vein 2 and is a smaller species; male is dark violet-blue. In the remaining Peninsula species (amphimuta, zylda), the HW band is not strongly dislocated at vein 2.

choices.label: Yes — HW postdiscal series dislocated at vein 2; smaller species; male dark violet-blue
No — HW band not strongly dislocated at vein 2

q_amph_s7.question: Is the male a warm violet-purple (with a reddish hue rather than blue), and is the FW postdiscal spot in space 3 an oblique oval?
q_amph_s7.hint: A. amphimuta amphimuta is violet-purple (warmer/redder than violet-blue) with the FW spot 3 usually an oblique oval, and the FW band slightly dislocated at vein 4. FW length 20–23 mm. A. zylda elioti is violet-blue, smaller (FW 16–17 mm), with female exceptionally narrow borders. Both are Peninsula species.

choices.label: Yes — warm violet-purple; FW spot 3 oblique oval; FW band slightly dislocated at vein 4
No — violet-blue (not warm purple); smaller (FW 16–17 mm); female exceptionally narrow-bordered

q_amph_geo.question: Is the specimen from Borneo (not the Malay Peninsula)?
q_amph_geo.hint: Three amphimuta subgroup members are Bornean endemics: A. alica (thread-like border), A. baluensis (lacks white spot in HW cilia space 1a), and A. dajagaka (female has thick borders and darker venation). The remaining 6 species at this point are Malay Peninsula species.

choices.label: Yes — Bornean specimen
No — Malay Peninsula specimen

q_amph_bor3.question: Is the male's black border reduced to a thread (very narrow), and is the male a dark, dull purple?
q_amph_bor3.hint: A. alica: thread-like border; male dark dull purple (distinctly duller than A. moolaiana's shining violet-blue); FW ~17 mm; female purple with broad black borders. A. baluensis and A. dajagaka both have a regular ~0.5 mm border and are dark purple-blue. If the upperside is not visible, select Cannot determine — the next question uses wing size instead.

choices.label: Yes — border thread-like; male dark dull purple; FW ~17 mm; female purple, broad borders
No — border regular ~0.5 mm; male dark purple-blue
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_amph_bor2.question: Is the specimen large (FW approximately 21–25 mm), or does the female have thick black borders with darker venation?
q_amph_bor2.hint: Size is the most accessible separator: A. dajagaka is distinctly larger (FW 21–25 mm); A. baluensis is smaller (FW 18–20 mm). Confirm with female characters: A. dajagaka female has thick black borders and noticeably darker venation. A. baluensis female has narrower-than-typical borders; HW cilia in space 1a lacks a white spot (rarely obscurely present). Note: both have male valva unilobate; genitalia dissection gives certain identification.

choices.label: Larger (FW 21–25 mm); female with thick black borders and darker venation
Smaller (FW 18–20 mm); female with narrower borders; HW cilia space 1a lacks white spot

r_delta.name: Arhopala delta
r_delta.note: The ONLY tailed member of the amphimuta subgroup — short tooth-like tail at vein 2. Male: deep violet with a narrow, regular black border. Part of the amphimuta subgroup (HW spots 5–7 in echelon; spots rounded).

features.On the forewing underside, is the basal: No — basal cell spot present on FW underside
features.Is there a distinct white patch on the h: No — no white patch on HW cilia

r_avathina_avathina.name: Arhopala avathina avathina
r_avathina_avathina.note: Tailless. Distinctive: FW underside lacks a basal cell spot (absent, not obscure). HW postdiscal band completely dislocated at vein 2. Otherwise similar to other amphimuta subgroup members.

r_norda.name: Arhopala norda
r_norda.note: Tailless. Distinguished from A. major by the small white patch on the HW cilia just above the tornal lobe, and by less rounded (more angular) postdiscal spots on the underside.

r_major_major.name: Arhopala major major
r_major_major.note: Tailless. Both sexes bluer than A. amphimuta above. Male: FW black border expands from 2.0 mm at tornus to 3.0 mm at apex. Underside similar to A. amphimuta. HW cilia often prolonged into a short tooth at vein 2.

r_stubbsi.name: Arhopala stubbsi
r_stubbsi.note: Tailless. Male: rather dull bluish-purple with a broad, even ~2.0 mm black border — the widest border in the amphimuta subgroup. HW cilia often prolonged at vein 2.

r_moolaiana_yajuna.name: Arhopala moolaiana yajuna
r_moolaiana_yajuna.note: Tailless. Male: bright shining violet-blue with a thread-like black border; FW 18–22 mm. Female: blue colour reaches the HW termen or very nearly so — black border is extremely narrow or absent at the termen (diagnostic; unique in the amphimuta group). Underside markings very similar to A. norda. HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Range: Malay Peninsula and south Myanmar.

r_alica.name: Arhopala alica
r_alica.note: Tailless. Bornean endemic. Male: dark, dull purple with a thread-like black border; FW ~17 mm. Female: purple with broad black borders (contrasts sharply with A. moolaiana female). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Distinguished from A. moolaiana by: darker/duller male colour, smaller size, broader female borders, and Bornean range.

r_sceva_indra.name: Arhopala sceva indra
r_sceva_indra.note: Tailless. A smaller species. HW underside postdiscal series is dislocated at vein 2 (distinctive within the amphimuta subgroup). Male: dark violet-blue.

r_amphimuta_amphimuta.name: Arhopala amphimuta amphimuta
r_amphimuta_amphimuta.note: Tailless. Male: dark bluish-purple with a regular 0.5–1.0 mm black border; FW 20–23 mm. HW cilia often prolonged into a very short tooth at vein 2 (may be inconspicuous in worn specimens). FW underside: postdiscal band slightly dislocated at vein 4; spot in space 3 usually an oblique oval. Female: lighter purple with typical broad black borders. HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (as in A. zylda).

r_zylda_elioti.name: Arhopala zylda elioti
r_zylda_elioti.note: Tailless. Male: shining blue with a slight violet tinge; border 1.0 mm; FW 16–17 mm. HW evenly rounded — no trace of a tooth at vein 2 (A. amphimuta has a short tooth). Female: paler blue with exceptionally narrow black borders (the narrowest in the amphimuta subgroup). HW postdiscal band only partially dislocated at vein 2 (as in A. amphimuta). Malay Peninsula.

r_dajagaka.name: Arhopala dajagaka
r_dajagaka.note: Tailless. Bornean endemic. Larger Bornean species (FW 21–25 mm). Male: dark bluish-purple with a thin black border. Female: THICK black borders and noticeably darker venation across the wings (diagnostic). Male valva unilobate; certain identification may require genitalia dissection.

r_baluensis.name: Arhopala baluensis
r_baluensis.note: Tailless. Bornean endemic. Smaller Bornean species (FW 18–20 mm). Male: dark purple-blue with a regular 0.5 mm border. Female: purple-blue with narrower-than-typical black borders. HW cilia in space 1a lacks a white spot (may be rarely present but obscure). Male valva unilobate; certain identification may require genitalia dissection.

q_muta_s1.question: Is the species noticeably small and duller bluish-purple on the male upperside?
q_muta_s1.hint: A. tropaea is distinctly smaller (FW ~12–14 mm) than A. moorei busa (FW 16–18 mm) and A. muta maranda (FW 17–19 mm), with a duller, less saturated bluish-purple male upperside. A. muta and A. moorei are larger, brighter blue.

choices.label: Yes — noticeably small; male duller bluish-purple
No — larger (FW 16–19 mm); male bright blue
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_muta_s2.question: On the male upperside, are both the forewing and hindwing exactly the same shade of blue in all lights?
q_muta_s2.hint: A. moorei busa: both wings the same shade of blue (bluish-purple) in all lights; female is duller and more purple than A. muta's female; FW 16–18 mm. A. muta maranda: FW is a rather dark, bright blue; HW is slightly paler and more glistening, so the two wings are NOT the same shade in all lights; FW border expands from 1.0–1.5 mm at tornus to 1.5–2.0 mm at apex; FW 17–19 mm.

choices.label: Yes — both wings same shade of blue in all lights; female more purple (FW 16–18 mm)
No — FW dark bright blue; HW slightly paler and more glistening; border expands toward apex (FW 17–19 mm)
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

r_tropaea.name: Arhopala tropaea
r_tropaea.note: Tailless. Very small species. Male: duller bluish-purple upperside (noticeably less bright than A. muta or A. moorei). Underside: postdiscal spots quadrate/banded; FW space 2 spot stepped toward termen (muta subgroup).

r_moorei_busa.name: Arhopala moorei busa
r_moorei_busa.note: Tailless. Male: both wings the same shade of bluish-purple in all lights (FW 16–18 mm). Female: duller and more purple than A. muta's female. Underside: basal spot present in FW cell; spots quadrate; FW space 2 spot stepped toward termen.

r_muta_maranda.name: Arhopala muta maranda
r_muta_maranda.note: Tailless. Male: FW rather dark bright blue; HW slightly paler and more glistening — the two wings are NOT the same shade in all lights (FW 17–19 mm). FW black border expands from 1.0–1.5 mm at tornus to 1.5–2.0 mm at apex. Female: shining blue with only a slight purple tint. Underside: basal spot present in FW cell; HW postdiscal band dislocated — inner edge of spot in space 2 in line with, or only slightly outside, the outer edge of spot 1b.

q_perimuta_s1.question: Is there a yellowish discal area on the hindwing underside with obscure markings and no tornal green scales?
q_perimuta_s1.hint: A. perimuta regina is easily recognized by the yellowish (not white or grey) discal area on the HW underside, markings obscure or almost absent, and tornal green scales completely absent. If HW underside is normal, continue to the next question.

choices.label: Yes — yellowish HW discal area; markings obscure; no tornal green scales
No — HW underside normal

q_perimuta_s2.question: Is the male hindwing upperside a brilliant greenish-blue, contrasting strongly with the dark shining violet-blue forewing?
q_perimuta_s2.hint: A. metamuta metamuta has a striking two-tone male: dark shining violet-blue FW (1.5 mm border) vs. brilliant greenish-blue HW — the most visually distinctive species in the perimuta subgroup. A. antimuta antimuta (the most abundant amphimuta-group species) lacks this contrast: male is uniformly deep violet-blue with the border reduced to a thread; HW underside spot 6 is placed below spot 7.

choices.label: Yes — HW brilliant greenish-blue, contrasting with dark shining violet-blue FW
No — uniformly deep violet-blue; border thread-like
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

r_perimuta_regina.name: Arhopala perimuta regina
r_perimuta_regina.note: Tailless; evenly rounded HW tornus with no tooth or tail at vein 2. Easily recognized: HW underside has a yellowish discal area with obscure or almost absent markings; tornal green scales completely absent. FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup).

r_metamuta_metamuta.name: Arhopala metamuta metamuta
r_metamuta_metamuta.note:
⚠ Extremely rare — verify carefully before confirming this identification.

Tailless; evenly rounded HW tornus with no tooth or tail at vein 2. Striking upperside: male has a brilliant greenish-blue HW that contrasts strongly with the dark, shining violet-blue FW (1.5 mm border). FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup). If any doubt remains about the contrasting HW colour, reconsider A. antimuta antimuta (the far more abundant perimuta-subgroup species).

r_antimuta_antimuta.name: Arhopala antimuta antimuta
r_antimuta_antimuta.note: The most abundant species in the amphimuta group. Tailless. Male: deep violet-blue; black border reduced to a thread. HW underside: postdiscal spot 6 placed below spot 7 (not beside it). FW postdiscal spots 2 and 3 are in line (perimuta subgroup). Confirmatory: male upperside uniformly coloured — no contrasting wing colour as in A. metamuta.

q_anthelus_s1.question: Does the forewing underside have at least 3 spots in space 11, AND does the hindwing underside have 2 subbasal spots in space 8?
q_anthelus_s1.hint: A. anthelus grahami (anthelus subgroup) has EXTRA spots — ≥3 in FW space 11 and 2 subbasal spots in HW space 8; spots on FW and upper HW half are deep purple-brown; FW 28 mm. A. achelous achelous (achelous subgroup) has NO spots in FW space 11 and only a single spot at the base of HW space 8; FW 22 mm.

choices.label: Yes — ≥3 spots in FW space 11; 2 subbasal spots in HW space 8; spots deep purple-brown
No — 0 spots in FW space 11; single spot at base of HW space 8

r_anthelus_grahami.name: Arhopala anthelus grahami
r_anthelus_grahami.note: One of the most beautiful in the genus. FW 28 mm. Underside: ≥3 spots in FW space 11; 2 spots at base of HW space 8; spots on FW and upper HW half deep purple-brown. Male: brilliant shining blue, narrow black border. Female: violet-blue with fairly broad black borders. Anthelus subgroup.

r_achelous_achelous.name: Arhopala achelous achelous
r_achelous_achelous.note: Smaller than A. anthelus grahami. FW 22 mm. Underside: NO spots in FW space 11; single spot at base of HW space 8 (lacks the extra spots of A. anthelus). Male: shining dark blue, narrow 0.5 mm black border. Subspecies A. achelous malu (Kedawi) lacks most of the purple tinge on the underside. Achelous subgroup.

q_aurea_s1.question: Is the underside glazed with a distinct purple wash?
q_aurea_s1.hint: A. trogon and A. stinga both have an underside glazed with a purple wash. A. aurea, A. caeca, and A. borneensis are hair-brown underneath (no purple glaze). This is the primary split within the aurea group.

choices.label: Yes — underside glazed with a purple wash
No — underside hair-brown (no purple glaze)

q_aurea_purple.question: Is the forewing black border significantly wide at the tornus — 2.5–3.0 mm?
q_aurea_purple.hint: A. stinga has an unusually wide FW border of 2.5–3.0 mm at the tornus (the widest in the group), decreasing to a thread near the apex; the male has no basal blue suffusion. A. trogon has a thread-like border throughout; male is brilliant brassy green with a strong blue suffusion at the wing bases; HW green usually reaches the termen in spaces 3, 4, or 5.

choices.label: Yes — FW border 2.5–3.0 mm at tornus; male green without blue base suffusion
No — FW border thread throughout; male brilliant brassy green with strong blue base suffusion

q_aurea_brown.question: Are the underside markings incomplete — lacking basal/central cell spots on the forewing and various basal/discal spots on the hindwing, with a narrow postdiscal band?
q_aurea_brown.hint: A. caeca has distinctively incomplete underside markings (FW lacks basal and central cell spots; HW lacks various basal and discal spots; narrow postdiscal band) and the male green turns to violet-blue at the tornus of both wings. A. aurea and A. borneensis have complete markings.

choices.label: Yes — incomplete markings; male green turns to violet-blue at tornus of both wings
No — complete underside markings

q_aurea_brown2.question: Is the forewing black border a thread throughout (even at the tornus), AND does the hindwing green narrowly reach the termen in spaces 5 and 6?
q_aurea_brown2.hint: A. borneensis (Bornean): FW border thread throughout, widening almost imperceptibly toward tornus; HW green reaches termen narrowly in spaces 5 and 6; male brassy green with strong blue base suffusion. A. aurea: FW border 0.75–1.5 mm at tornus, decreasing to a thread above vein 3 or 4; HW green does NOT reach the termen; male green with only weak blue base suffusion; female blue to purple-blue.

choices.label: Yes — FW border thread throughout; HW green reaches termen in spaces 5–6; strong blue base (Bornean)
No — FW border 0.75–1.5 mm at tornus, decreasing to thread; HW green not reaching termen; weak blue base

r_aurea.name: Arhopala aurea
r_aurea.note: Underside hair-brown with complete markings. Male: shining green with weak blue suffusion at wing bases; FW border 0.75–1.5 mm at tornus, decreasing to a thread above vein 3 or 4; HW green does not reach the termen. Female: blue to purple-blue with a relatively narrow HW border. Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm).

r_trogon.name: Arhopala trogon
r_trogon.note: Underside glazed with a purple wash. Male: brilliant brassy green with a strong blue suffusion at the wing bases; FW border a thread throughout; HW green usually reaches the termen in spaces 3, 4, or 5. Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm).

r_stinga.name: Arhopala stinga
r_stinga.note: Underside glazed with a purple wash (exactly as in A. trogon). Male: green WITHOUT any basal blue suffusion (no blue at wing bases); FW border distinctively wide — 2.5–3.0 mm at tornus, decreasing to a thread near the apex (widest border in the group). Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm).

r_caeca.name: Arhopala caeca
r_caeca.note: Underside hair-brown with INCOMPLETE markings: FW lacks basal and central cell spots; HW lacks various basal and discal spots; postdiscal band narrow. Male: brilliant green turning to violet-blue at the tornus of both wings (diagnostic — unique in the group); FW border a thread. Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm).

r_borneensis.name: Arhopala borneensis
r_borneensis.note: Bornean taxon. Underside hair-brown with complete markings (as A. aurea). Male: brassy green with strong blue suffusion at base; FW border thread throughout, widening almost imperceptibly toward tornus; HW green reaches termen narrowly in spaces 5 and 6 (unlike A. aurea where green does not reach termen). Short stumpy HW tail (~2 mm).

q_camdeo_c1.question: Is there a central spot in forewing underside space 11? (camdeo group)
q_camdeo_c1.hint: A. anarte is one of the largest and most beautiful species in the genus, easily recognized by a central spot in FW space 11. Male: shining pale silvery blue overlaid with violet toward the margins. No other camdeo subgroup member has this spot.

choices.label: Yes — central spot in FW space 11; male shining pale silvery blue with violet margins
No — no spot in FW space 11

q_camdeo_c2.question: Is the underside ground colour extensively whitened?
q_camdeo_c2.hint: A. dispar pendleburyi is immediately distinctive for its extensively whitened underside ground colour. Male: pale blue with a thread-like border; FW cell-end also whitened. All other camdeo subgroup members have a normal brown/dark underside.

choices.label: Yes — underside extensively whitened; male pale blue, thread border, whitened FW cell-end
No — underside ground colour normal (not whitened)

q_camdeo_c3.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band incomplete, ending at or before vein 7 (lower part absent)?
q_camdeo_c3.hint: A. belphoebe cowani and A. johoreana johoreana both have an incomplete FW postdiscal band. All other camdeo subgroup members have a complete (or nearly complete) band.

choices.label: Yes — FW postdiscal band incomplete, ending at vein 7
No — FW postdiscal band complete (or only partially dislocated at vein 4)

q_camdeo_incomplete.question: Is the hindwing tail completely absent (tailless)?
q_camdeo_incomplete.hint: Tail presence alone separates these two species — no upperside needed. A. belphoebe cowani is completely tailless; both sexes have broad dark borders and pale violet-blue upperside. A. johoreana johoreana has a short hindwing tail and is much darker violet-blue.

choices.label: Yes — tailless (no HW tail)
No — short HW tail present
Cannot determine — tail condition unclear

q_camdeo_c4.question: Is the hindwing tail very short (≤2 mm) AND are the underside spots indistinct?
q_camdeo_c4.hint: A. hellada ozana and A. varro selama both have a very short HW tail and relatively indistinct underside spots. A. hellada: FW spot 4 shifted distad; clear violet-blue male. A. varro: small species; very short tail ~1.5 mm; even pale metallic silvery blue male.

choices.label: Yes — HW tail very short (≤2 mm); underside spots indistinct
No — tail of normal length or absent; underside spots distinct

q_camdeo_short_tail.question: Is the forewing postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (outward, out of line with spots 5 and 6)?
q_camdeo_short_tail.hint: A. hellada ozana: FW spot 4 shifted distad (out of line); very short HW tail; male clear violet-blue with thread-like border. A. varro selama: FW spot 4 not shifted; small species; male an even pale metallic silvery blue; HW tail very short (~1.5 mm).

choices.label: Yes — FW spot 4 shifted distad; male clear violet-blue, thread border
No — FW spot 4 in normal position; small species; male even pale metallic silvery blue

q_camdeo_c5.question: Is the male a deep, dull bluish-purple with a forewing border expanding to 1.5 mm at the apex?
q_camdeo_c5.hint: A. semperi russelli has a distinctive deep, dull bluish-purple male (not clear violet) and a FW border that expands from thread-width to 1.5 mm at the apex, with a large cell on the underside. If the male upperside is not visible, choose 'Cannot determine' — HW spot 6 (next question) can still separate the remaining candidates, but A. semperi russelli may be missed.

choices.label: Yes — deep dull bluish-purple; border expands to 1.5 mm at apex; large rare species
No — clear violet-blue with narrow borders
Cannot determine — upperside not visible (note: may miss semperi russelli)

q_camdeo_c6.question: On the hindwing underside, is postdiscal spot 6 smaller than postdiscal spot 7?
q_camdeo_c6.hint: A. camdana camdana: HW postdiscal spot 6 is noticeably smaller than spot 7; male clear violet-blue. A. opalina azata: HW spot 6 is NOT smaller than spot 7; male pale greyish-blue with a violet tinge and very narrow borders; FW band complete, partially dislocated at vein 4.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 smaller than spot 7; male clear violet-blue
No — HW spot 6 not smaller than spot 7; male pale greyish-blue with violet tinge

r_anarte.name: Arhopala anarte
r_anarte.note: One of the most beautiful and largest in the genus. Immediately recognized by the central spot in FW underside space 11 (no other camdeo subgroup member has this). Male: shining pale silvery blue overlaid with violet toward the margins. Camdeo subgroup.

r_dispar_pendleburyi.name: Arhopala dispar pendleburyi
