r_dispar_pendleburyi.note: Distinctive for its extensively whitened underside ground colour (diagnostic). Male: pale blue with a thread-like border; FW cell-end also whitened. Camdeo subgroup.

r_belphoebe_cowani.name: Arhopala belphoebe cowani
r_belphoebe_cowani.note: Tailless. FW postdiscal band incomplete, ending at vein 7. Both sexes alike: broad dark borders and pale violet-blue upperside. Camdeo subgroup.

r_johoreana_johoreana.name: Arhopala johoreana johoreana
r_johoreana_johoreana.note: Similar to A. belphoebe cowani (incomplete FW postdiscal band) but possesses a short tail and is much darker violet-blue. Camdeo subgroup.

r_hellada_ozana.name: Arhopala hellada ozana
r_hellada_ozana.note: Very short HW tail; underside spots indistinct. FW postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (out of line with spots 5 and 6). Male: clear violet-blue with thread-like border. Camdeo subgroup.

r_varro_selama.name: Arhopala varro selama
r_varro_selama.note: Small species. Very short HW tail (~1.5 mm). Male: even, pale metallic silvery blue upperside. FW spot 4 in normal position (not shifted). Camdeo subgroup.

r_semperi_russelli.name: Arhopala semperi russelli
r_semperi_russelli.note: Large and very rare. Male: deep, dull bluish-purple; FW border expands to 1.5 mm at the apex. Female: purple, notably whitish at the end-cell. Camdeo subgroup.

r_camdana_camdana.name: Arhopala camdana camdana
r_camdana_camdana.note: Closely related to A. opalina. Male: clear violet-blue. HW underside: postdiscal spot 6 is smaller than spot 7 (diagnostic vs A. opalina). Camdeo subgroup.

r_opalina_azata.name: Arhopala opalina azata
r_opalina_azata.note: FW postdiscal band complete, only partially dislocated at vein 4. Male: pale greyish-blue with a violet tinge and very narrow borders. Female: slightly deeper blue. HW spot 6 not smaller than spot 7 (unlike A. camdana). Camdeo subgroup.

q_camdeo_m1.question: Is the specimen tailed?
q_camdeo_m1.hint: A. myrzala lammas is tailed; male shining royal blue with narrow black borders. A. myrzalina is tailless; male has a broad FW border expanding from 1.5 mm at the tornus to 4.0 mm at the costa. Both have the distinctive crimson-brown underside with white striae and a HW space 6 basal spot.

choices.label: Yes — tailed; male shining royal blue, narrow borders
No — tailless; male broad FW border (1.5 mm at tornus → 4.0 mm at costa)

r_myrzala_lammas.name: Arhopala myrzala lammas
r_myrzala_lammas.note: Tailed. Underside: crimson-brown with markings outlined by white striae; spot at base of HW space 6. Male: shining royal blue with narrow black borders. Myrzala subgroup.

r_myrzalina.name: Arhopala myrzalina
r_myrzalina.note: Tailless. Underside: crimson-brown with markings outlined by white striae; spot at base of HW space 6. Male: broad FW border expanding from 1.5 mm at tornus to 4.0 mm at costa. Myrzala subgroup.

r_bazaloides_bazaloides.name: Arhopala bazaloides bazaloides
r_bazaloides_bazaloides.note: Underside: purple-brown with markings outlined by white striae; extra spot in mid-space 8 on HW underside (diagnostic for subgroup); NO spot at base of HW space 6 (unlike myrzala subgroup). Male: dark violet-blue with regular 2.0 mm black borders. Bazaloides subgroup.

q_centaurus_amantes.question: Does the male have very broad black borders (3.0 mm at tornus expanding to 5.0 mm at apex), AND is the FW postdiscal spot in space 4 shifted distad (out of line)?
q_centaurus_amantes.hint: A. amantes (South Myanmar) is keyed here due to similar silvery FW markings but is NOT in the centaurus group. It has the broadest borders in this section and the space 4 FW spot clearly shifted outward; it also lacks a costal spot in FW space 10. A. centaurus and A. araxes both have thread-like or narrow borders (≤1 mm). If the male upperside is not visible, choose Cannot determine — A. amantes is unlikely outside South Myanmar.

choices.label: Yes — borders 3.0–5.0 mm widening toward apex; FW spot 4 shifted distad; no FW space 10 costal spot (South Myanmar)
No — borders thread-like or ≤1 mm
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_centaurus_s1.question: Does the hindwing have a distinct tornal lobe?
q_centaurus_s1.hint: A. araxes has a distinct tornal lobe on the HW; male shining blue with violet toward apices; FW border increases from a thread at tornus to 1.0 mm at apex; FW ~27 mm; Sumatra to Sulawesi. A. centaurus nakula has a rounded HW tornus with a vestigial (very small or absent) tornal lobe; male purplish blue, thread border; FW 28–29 mm.

choices.label: Yes — distinct HW tornal lobe; male shining blue with violet toward apices; FW ~27 mm (Sumatra–Sulawesi)
No — HW tornus rounded, tornal lobe vestigial or absent; male purplish blue; FW 28–29 mm

r_centaurus_nakula.name: Arhopala centaurus nakula
r_centaurus_nakula.note: FW 28–29 mm. HW tornus rounded; tornal lobe vestigial or absent. Male: purplish blue, FW border reduced to a thread. Female: paler and more purple than male; FW border more intensely darkened over distal half of cell; moderately broad black borders. Underside: FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver.

r_araxes.name: Arhopala araxes
r_araxes.note: FW ~27 mm. HW with a distinct tornal lobe (unlike A. centaurus). Male: shining blue with violet tinge toward apices; FW border a thread at tornus, increasing to 1.0 mm at apex. Female: blue. Range: Sumatra to Sulawesi region. Underside: FW cell spots outlined in greenish silver.

r_amantes.name: Arhopala amantes
r_amantes.note: Keyed here due to similar silvery FW cell markings, but technically outside the centaurus group. FW ~27 mm. Male: shining blue, very broad borders — 3.0 mm at tornus expanding to 5.0 mm at apex. Female: lighter blue. Underside: FW spot 4 shifted distad; lacks costal spot in FW space 10. Range: South Myanmar.

r_agaba.name: Arhopala agaba
r_agaba.note: Agaba subgroup — unique in the cleander group. HW underside: postdiscal spot 5 contiguous or amalgamated with the end-cell bar. HW postdiscal band only PARTIALLY dislocated at vein 2 (all other cleander group members have it completely dislocated). Large prominent HW tornal lobe.

features.Is the postdiscal band placed very close: No — postdiscal band not especially close to submarginal lunules

q_cleander_s1.question: Is the forewing postdiscal band NOT dislocated at vein 4 — straight through or only slightly sinuous?
q_cleander_s1.hint: A. cleander aphadantas: FW band not dislocated at vein 4 (straight or only sinuous); male violet-blue with broad black borders. All other cleander subgroup members have the FW band partially dislocated at vein 4.

choices.label: Yes — FW band not dislocated at vein 4 (straight/sinuous); male violet-blue, broad borders
No — FW band partially dislocated at vein 4

q_cleander_s2.question: Is the forewing postdiscal spot in space 9 as wide as or wider than the spot in space 6?
q_cleander_s2.hint: A. zambra zambra: FW spot 9 ≥ width of spot 6; male typically deep blue. In A. athada and A. silhetensis, FW spot 9 is narrower than spot 6 and shifted basad.

choices.label: Yes — FW spot 9 as wide as or wider than spot 6; male deep blue
No — FW spot 9 narrower than spot 6, shifted basad

q_cleander_s3.question: Does hindwing postdiscal spot 6 completely fill the gap between spot 5 and the end-cell bar (no gap visible between spot 6 and the bar)?
q_cleander_s3.hint: A. athada athada: HW spot 6 fills the gap between spot 5 and the end-cell bar (spot 6 is large, touching or almost touching the bar). A. silhetensis adorea: gap between HW spot 6 and the end-cell bar is WIDER than spot 6 itself.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 fills the gap; spot 6 touches or nearly touches the end-cell bar
No — gap between HW spot 6 and end-cell bar is wider than spot 6

r_cleander_aphadantas.name: Arhopala cleander aphadantas
r_cleander_aphadantas.note: FW postdiscal band NOT dislocated at vein 4 (straight or only sinuous). Male: violet-blue with broad black borders. Large species (FW > 21 mm); conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe. HW band completely dislocated at vein 2.

r_zambra_zambra.name: Arhopala zambra zambra
r_zambra_zambra.note: FW postdiscal band partially dislocated at vein 4. FW postdiscal spot 9 as wide as or wider than spot 6. Male: typically deep blue. Large species (FW > 21 mm); conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe.

r_athada_athada.name: Arhopala athada athada
r_athada_athada.note: FW postdiscal spot 9 narrower than spot 6, shifted basad. HW postdiscal spot 6 fills the gap between spot 5 and the end-cell bar. Large species (FW > 21 mm); conspicuously projecting HW tornal lobe.

r_silhetensis_adorea.name: Arhopala silhetensis adorea
r_silhetensis_adorea.note: Similar to A. athada. FW postdiscal spot 9 narrower than spot 6, shifted basad. HW postdiscal spot 6 does NOT fill the gap — the gap between HW spot 6 and the end-cell bar is wider than spot 6 itself. Large species (FW > 21 mm).

q_alea_s1.question: Is the underside pale slatey purple with conspicuous dark markings outlined by nearly-white striae?
q_alea_s1.hint: A. aurelia is immediately distinctive for its pale slatey-purple ground colour with dark markings strongly outlined by nearly-white striae. No other alea subgroup member has this combination.

choices.label: Yes — pale slatey purple underside; dark markings outlined by nearly-white striae
No

q_alea_s2.question: Does the underside have a slatey pink glaze with striae that are whiter than in other species?
q_alea_s2.hint: A. evansi has a slatey pink glaze on the underside and the striae are noticeably whiter than other alea subgroup members. If the underside is predominantly brown (not pink-glazed), choose No.

choices.label: Yes — slatey pink glaze; striae noticeably white
No — no slatey pink glaze

q_alea_s3.question: Are the wings notably rounded and elongate in shape?
q_alea_s3.hint: A. aroa aroa has distinctively rounded, elongate wings — a recognizable shape character. It also has narrow postdiscal bands that are well clear of the submarginal lunules. All other alea subgroup members have more typical Arhopala wing shapes.

choices.label: Yes — wings notably rounded and elongate; narrow bands well clear of submarginal lunules
No — typical wing shape

q_alea_s4.question: Is the postdiscal band placed very close to the inner submarginal lunules?
q_alea_s4.hint: A. phanda phanda is distinguished by having the postdiscal band unusually close to the inner submarginal lunules on the underside. In all other remaining alea subgroup members, the band is further from the lunules.

choices.label: Yes — postdiscal band very close to inner submarginal lunules
No — postdiscal band not especially close to submarginal lunules

q_alea_s5.question: Does hindwing postdiscal spot 6 widely overlap the end-cell bar?
q_alea_s5.hint: A. selta selta: HW spot 6 widely overlaps the end-cell bar; male violet-blue with border expanding toward apex. A. phaenops sandakani is similar to A. selta but HW spot 6 usually does NOT overlap the end-cell bar.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 widely overlaps the end-cell bar; male violet-blue, border expanding toward apex
No — HW spot 6 does not widely overlap the end-cell bar

q_alea_s6.question: Is the male border noticeably expanding toward the apex (similar to A. selta)?
q_alea_s6.hint: A. phaenops sandakani is very similar to A. selta in border pattern (border expanding toward apex) but differs in that HW spot 6 does not overlap the end-cell bar. The remaining species (sublustris, milleri, normani) have narrow or thread-like borders that do NOT expand toward the apex.

choices.label: Yes — border expanding toward apex; HW spot 6 not overlapping end-cell bar
No — border narrow or thread-like throughout, not expanding toward apex
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

q_alea_s7.question: Is the male a bright, glistening blue (not matt or indigo)?
q_alea_s7.hint: A. sublustris ridleyi and A. normani are both bright-ish blue with very narrow borders. A. milleri is larger, with a more matt indigo-blue colour and a thread-like border. If the specimen is noticeably large and the blue is matt/flat (not glistening), choose No.

choices.label: Yes — bright glistening blue (not matt); borders under 0.5 mm
No — matt indigo blue; larger; thread-like border

q_alea_glist.question: Is the male less shining and more violet (rather than purely bright blue)?
q_alea_glist.hint: A. normani is superficially similar to A. sublustris ridleyi but the male is less shining and has a more violet tone. A. sublustris ridleyi has a bright glistening blue with borders under 0.5 mm.

choices.label: Yes — less shining; more violet than blue
No — bright glistening blue, borders under 0.5 mm
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

r_aurelia.name: Arhopala aurelia
r_aurelia.note: Alea subgroup. Distinctive underside: pale slatey purple with conspicuous dark markings outlined by nearly-white striae. Smaller species (FW < 21 mm); less conspicuous HW tornal lobe.

r_evansi.name: Arhopala evansi
r_evansi.note: Alea subgroup. Distinctive underside: slatey pink glaze with striae that are whiter than in other alea subgroup members. Smaller species (FW < 21 mm).

r_aroa_aroa.name: Arhopala aroa aroa
r_aroa_aroa.note: Alea subgroup. Recognizable by its rounded, elongate wings (distinctive wing shape). Narrow postdiscal bands that are well clear of the submarginal lunules. Smaller species (FW < 21 mm).

r_phanda_phanda.name: Arhopala phanda phanda
r_phanda_phanda.note: Alea subgroup. Underside postdiscal band placed very close to the inner submarginal lunules (diagnostic). Smaller species (FW < 21 mm).

r_selta_selta.name: Arhopala selta selta
r_selta_selta.note: Alea subgroup. HW postdiscal spot 6 widely overlaps the end-cell bar (diagnostic vs phaenops). Male: violet-blue with border expanding toward the apex. Smaller species (FW < 21 mm).

r_phaenops_sandakani.name: Arhopala phaenops sandakani
r_phaenops_sandakani.note: Alea subgroup. Similar to A. selta selta (border expanding toward apex) but HW postdiscal spot 6 usually does NOT overlap the end-cell bar. Smaller species (FW < 21 mm).

r_milleri.name: Arhopala milleri
r_milleri.note: Alea subgroup. Larger than A. sublustris (but still FW < 21 mm). Male: more matt indigo blue (not bright glistening) with a thread-like border. Genitalia often needed for certain identification.

r_normani.name: Arhopala normani
r_normani.note: Alea subgroup. Superficially similar to A. sublustris ridleyi but male is less shining and more violet. Borders narrow. Genitalia often needed for certain identification.

r_sublustris_ridleyi.name: Arhopala sublustris ridleyi
r_sublustris_ridleyi.note: Alea subgroup. Male: bright glistening blue with borders under 0.5 mm. Brighter and more glistening than A. normani. Smaller species (FW < 21 mm).

r_corinda_acestes.name: Arhopala corinda acestes
r_corinda_acestes.note: Monotypic group. Filamentous HW tail ~3.5 mm. FW: pointed apex, straight termen. Underside: large purple-brown patch under FW cell (basal half of space 1b completely darkened); FW spot 4 shifted distad; no costal spot in FW space 10; ground colour sometimes glazed with purple wash. HW spot 6 outwardly convex; band partially dislocated at vein 2. Male: deep violet or dark purple, very narrow border. Female: purple-blue. Subspecies: acestes (Malay Peninsula and Singapore), corestes (Langkawi).

features.What is the underside ground colour and: Underside sometimes with purple wash; dark patch under FW cell; pointed FW apex, straight termen; smaller (tail ~3.5 mm) — A. corinda

q_democritus_s1.question: Is the underside ground colour a dark chocolate brown with markings outlined by prominent silvery-white striae and dots?
q_democritus_s1.hint: A. democritus democritus (Kedawi and Fraser's Hill): darker chocolate-brown ground colour; markings outlined by prominent silvery-white striae and dots; male and female both lighter and more silvery blue above. A. democritus lycaenaria (standard Peninsula subspecies): hair-brown ground colour; markings outlined by dull yellowish-white striae (less contrast).

choices.label: Yes — dark chocolate brown; prominent silvery-white striae; male/female more silvery blue (Kedawi/Fraser's Hill)
No — hair brown; dull yellowish-white striae (standard Peninsula subspecies)

r_democritus_democritus.name: Arhopala democritus democritus
r_democritus_democritus.note: Underside: dark chocolate-brown ground colour; markings outlined by prominent silvery-white striae and dots (more contrast than lycaenaria). Upperside: both male and female lighter and more silvery blue. Distribution: Kedawi; also recorded at Fraser's Hill.

r_democritus_lycaenaria.name: Arhopala democritus lycaenaria
r_democritus_lycaenaria.note: Standard Malay Peninsula subspecies. Underside: hair-brown ground colour; all markings outlined by dull yellowish-white striae (less contrast than democritus ssp.). Short HW tail 1.5–2.0 mm. Macular underside markings; FW spot 4 shifted distad; HW spot 6 outwardly concave; HW band widely dislocated at vein 2.

q_epimuta_s1.question: Does the hindwing have a short tail (~2.5 mm)?
q_epimuta_s1.hint: Two species in this group (atosia, lurida) have a short hindwing tail; epimuta is tailless.

choices.label: Yes — short tail present (~2.5 mm)
No tail — hindwing untailed

q_epimuta_s2.question: How do the underside markings contrast with the ground colour?
q_epimuta_s2.hint: Both tailed Epimuta species share a similar underside pattern but differ in contrast. A. atosia malayana: markings blend into the ground colour (feebly contrasting). A. lurida: markings are distinctly darker and more sharply defined.

choices.label: Markings contrast feebly — pattern blends into ground colour
Markings darker and more clearly defined
Cannot determine — underside also not visible

r_atosia_malayana.name: Arhopala atosia malayana
r_atosia_malayana.note: HW tail ~2.5 mm. Male FW upperside: thread-width black border; discal patch large and circular, extending widely into the cell and space 1b. Underside markings contrast feebly with the ground colour.

r_lurida.name: Arhopala lurida
r_lurida.note: HW tail ~2.5 mm. Male FW upperside: border slightly expanding toward apex; discal patch smaller than in A. atosia, almost entirely confined to bases of spaces 2 and 3. Underside markings darker and more clearly defined than A. atosia.

r_epimuta_tailed_unclear.name: Epimuta group — tailed species (unresolved)
r_epimuta_tailed_unclear.note:
⚠ Neither underside nor upperside visible. Both candidates have HW tail ~2.5 mm.

Primary character (underside): A. atosia malayana — markings contrast feebly with ground colour. A. lurida — markings darker and more clearly defined.

Confirmatory character (upperside, male): A. atosia — FW discal patch large and circular, extending widely into cell and space 1b; border a thread. A. lurida — patch smaller, almost confined to bases of spaces 2–3; border slightly expanding toward apex.

Check the underside contrast first; inspect upperside if still uncertain.

r_epimuta_epiala.name: Arhopala epimuta epiala
r_epimuta_epiala.note: Tailless. Male FW upperside: large circular discal patch similar to A. atosia, but distinguished by the complete absence of a hindwing tail.

q_eumolphus_s1.question: How long is the hindwing tail?
q_eumolphus_s1.hint: Measure the tail at vein 2. overdijkinki has a distinctly longer tail (~5 mm) compared with all other species in the group (~3 mm).

choices.label: Tail ~5 mm (markedly long)
Tail ~3 mm

q_eumolphus_blue.question: Is the underside ground colour purplish-brown, with the forewing apex dusted with pale violet scales?
q_eumolphus_blue.hint: This character is visible on the underside and does not require seeing the upperside. In A. bazalus the overall underside is purplish-brown (not the usual hair-brown) and the FW apex is visibly powdered with pale violet scales.

choices.label: Yes — purplish-brown underside; FW apex with pale violet powder
No — hair-brown underside; no violet powder at apex

r_overdijkinki_unda.name: Arhopala overdijkinki unda
r_overdijkinki_unda.note: HW tail 5.0 mm (longest in group). Male: rich violet; FW black border 2.5 mm at tornus, expanding strongly to 7.0 mm at costa. Female: shining blue with evenly curved FW border.

r_bazalus_zalinda.name: Arhopala bazalus zalinda
r_bazalus_zalinda.note: HW tail ~3 mm. Male: deep purple; FW black border 1.0 mm at apex decreasing to 1.5 mm at tornus. Underside purplish-brown with very dark markings; FW apex powdered with pale violet scales. Female: usually purple-blue.

r_acta.name: Arhopala acta
r_acta.note: HW tail 3.0 mm. Male: light violet-blue with a regular, very narrow border. FW rounded. Underside markings faint. Female not described.

r_tameanga.name: Arhopala tameanga
r_tameanga.note: HW tail 3.0 mm. Male: dark violet-blue; FW apex acute, termen nearly straight. Female: purple-blue with HW border ≥2.0 mm wide. Underside markings clearly defined. Bornean endemic.

q_eumolphus_green.question: Is the forewing termen evenly curved (rounded apex), or straight and slightly concave (acute apex)?
q_eumolphus_green.hint: This can be assessed from either side. A rounded apex has a smooth, evenly curved termen. An acute apex comes to a more pointed tip, often with a straight or slightly concave termen below it.

choices.label: FW apex rounded; termen evenly curved
FW apex acute; termen straight or slightly concave

q_eumolphus_rounded.question: How distinct are the underside markings?
q_eumolphus_rounded.hint: Three species share a rounded FW and ~3 mm tail. They differ in underside contrast: acta has very faint markings; horsfieldi has unusually dark, broad markings; eumolphus maxwelli is intermediate (standard contrast). If the upperside is visible: acta = blue/violet; horsfieldi = green with very broad borders reaching the cell; eumolphus = brassy green with narrow borders.

choices.label: Underside markings very faint or obscure
Underside markings very dark and broad
Underside markings standard contrast

q_eumolphus_acute.question: Where was this specimen found?
q_eumolphus_acute.hint: Both acute-apex species are similar; geography is the most reliable separator when the upperside is not visible. tameanga is a Bornean endemic; hellenore occurs on the Peninsula. If the upperside is visible: hellenore has a thread-width border above vein 4; tameanga has a broader border.

choices.label: Borneo (Sabah / Sarawak / Brunei)
Malay Peninsula (or unknown locality)

r_horsfieldi_basiviridis.name: Arhopala horsfieldi basiviridis
r_horsfieldi_basiviridis.note: Male: green with very broad black borders reaching the cell on all wings. Underside generally darker than relatives, with broad markings. Female: more purple, with slightly broader borders than A. eumolphus.

r_eumolphus_maxwelli.name: Arhopala eumolphus maxwelli
r_eumolphus_maxwelli.note: Male: brassy green; FW rounded; black border 2.0 mm at tornus decreasing to 1.0 mm at apex. Female: purple-blue; black border usually reaches apex of FW cell.

r_hellenore_siroes.name: Arhopala hellenore siroes
r_hellenore_siroes.note: Male: similar to A. eumolphus but border reduced to a thread above vein 4; FW apex more acute; termen straight or slightly concave. Female: purple-blue with narrower borders than A. eumolphus, not reaching apex of FW cell.

r_fulla_intaca.name: Arhopala fulla intaca
r_fulla_intaca.note: Tailless. FW ~18 mm. Instantly recognizable by the near-total absence of underside markings: no end-cell bar on FW, postdiscal spots replaced by an obscure fascia, only a few basal spots near HW base. Male: shining violet-blue with thread-width border. Female: deeper violet with broader borders. Wide distribution: Myanmar to Sundaland, Philippines, Moluccas, New Guinea.

q_ganesa_s1.question: Are tornal green scales present on the hindwing underside?
q_ganesa_s1.hint: Check the tornal area (lower hind angle) of the hindwing underside for metallic green scales. Two ganesa species lack them (paraganesa, ammonides); three have them (ammon, ariel, elizabethae).

choices.label: No tornal green scales
Tornal green scales present

q_ganesa_no_green.question: Is there a roughly quadrate whitish area on the hindwing underside extending from mid-cell to the costa?
q_ganesa_no_green.hint: In paraganesa this whitish mid-cell patch is distinctive and quite obvious. ammonides lacks it; instead the gap between the outer and central spots in HW space 7 is completely whitened. paraganesa is also wholly montane on the Peninsula.

choices.label: Yes — roughly quadrate whitish area from mid-cell to costa
No — gap between HW space 7 spots whitened, but no mid-cell white patch

q_ganesa_with_green.question: Does the postdiscal spot in hindwing space 6 extend all the way to the base of the space?
q_ganesa_with_green.hint: In A. ammon the space 6 spot is unusually elongated, reaching the base of the space. In ariel and elizabethae spot 6 is normal and does not reach the base.

choices.label: Yes — HW spot 6 extended to base of space
No — HW spot 6 normal, not reaching base

q_ganesa_ariel_eliz.question: What is the male upperside appearance?
q_ganesa_ariel_eliz.hint: ⚠ Underside characters alone are insufficient to separate these two species — both have tornal green scales, normal spot 6, and spots 7 joined at their upper ends. Upperside required: ariel is bluish-purple and hardly shining with a border expanding from 1.5 mm at the tornus to 4.0 mm at the apex; elizabethae is shining deep blue with borders slightly wider throughout. If the upperside is not visible, choose 'Cannot determine'.

choices.label: Bluish-purple, hardly shining; FW border expanding to ~4 mm at apex
Shining deep blue; borders slightly wider than ariel
Cannot determine — upperside not visible

r_paraganesa_mendava.name: Arhopala paraganesa mendava
r_paraganesa_mendava.note: Montane species on the Malay Peninsula. Underside HW: roughly quadrate whitish area from mid-cell to costa; no tornal green scales. Upperside: bright pale blue with broad borders; male HW mostly fuscous; female with obscure whitish patch at FW end-cell.

r_ammonides_chunsu.name: Arhopala ammonides chunsu
r_ammonides_chunsu.note: No tornal green scales. Underside HW: gap between outer and central spots in space 7 completely whitened (no quadrate mid-cell white patch). Upperside: powdery blue with very broad borders on all wings; female with dark bar at FW end-cell.

r_ammon_ammon.name: Arhopala ammon ammon
r_ammon_ammon.note: Tornal green scales present. Underside HW: spot 6 extended to base of space; outer and central spots in space 7 separate, gap whitened. Upperside male: dark blue, narrow borders (1.5 mm at FW apex). Female: narrow borders (2.0 mm at FW tornus and HW).

r_ariel.name: Arhopala ariel
r_ariel.note: Tornal green scales present. Underside HW: spot 6 normal (not reaching base of space). ⚠ Upperside required to separate from elizabethae: male bluish-purple, hardly shining; FW border 1.5 mm at tornus expanding to 4.0 mm at apex. Female: bluer shade with broader borders.

r_elizabethae.name: Arhopala elizabethae
r_elizabethae.note: Tornal green scales present. Underside HW: spot 6 normal (not reaching base of space). ⚠ Upperside required to separate from ariel: male shining deep blue with borders slightly wider than A. ariel; female similar to male (not bluer with broader borders as in ariel female).

r_ariel_or_elizabethae.name: Ganesa group — ariel or elizabethae (unresolved)
r_ariel_or_elizabethae.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without male FW upperside. Both candidates share: tornal green scales, HW spot 6 normal (not reaching base of space).

A. ariel: male BLUISH-PURPLE, hardly shining; FW border 1.5 mm at tornus expanding to 4.0 mm at apex. Female bluer with broader borders than male.

A. elizabethae: male SHINING DEEP BLUE; borders slightly wider than ariel throughout. Female similar to male.

Inspect the upperside to confirm species.

r_hypomuta_hypomuta.name: Arhopala hypomuta hypomuta
r_hypomuta_hypomuta.note: Tailless. FW ~19 mm. Key character: small spot at the extreme base of HW space 6 on the underside (usually present in Malayan specimens; often absent in Bornean/Sumatran material). Similar to amphimuta group but separated by this basal spot. Male: deep shining violet-blue with very narrow borders. Female: more purple-blue with broader borders.

r_ijanensis.name: Arhopala ijanensis
r_ijanensis.note: Tailed. FW ~23 mm. HW cell about half wing length or longer. Underside: markings rather variegated; whitish streak from base of dorsum to apex on HW. Male: pale blue; FW costal border runs narrowly along costa to wing base (unique). Female: same but costal border much broader. More common in Kedah than elsewhere in the Peninsula.

features.Is there a whitish streak on the hindwin: Yes — whitish streak present on HW underside from dorsal base to apex

r_inornata_inornata.name: Arhopala inornata inornata
r_inornata_inornata.note: Tailless. FW ~21 mm. Very rounded HW tornus. Underside: no tornal green scales; HW postdiscal spot 6 placed immediately below spot 7. Male: bright violet-blue with border reduced to a thread. Female: bluer than male with exceptionally narrow borders (0.5 mm on HW).

r_allata_pandora.name: Arhopala allata pandora
r_allata_pandora.note: Tailed at vein 2. FW ~22 mm. FW apex acute; termen straight between veins 2–4. Underside postdiscal band sinuous or only slightly dislocated at vein 4; HW postdiscal spots narrow, regular, rectangular. Male: dark purple; FW border 0.5 mm at tornus expanding to 1.5 mm at apex. Female: dark shining blue. Rare; recorded from NE India to Neomalaya and Philippines.

q_rama_s1.question: Does the butterfly have a hindwing tail?
q_rama_s1.hint: One of the three Malayan rama species (arvina) is tailless. The other two are tailed with different tail lengths.

choices.label: Tailless — re-check first question
Tailed

q_rama_s2.question: How long is the hindwing tail, and are tornal green scales present?
q_rama_s2.hint: paralea has a longer tail (2.5 mm), pale purplish-brown underside, and no or vestigial tornal green scales; it is montane on the Peninsula. buddha has a very short tail (~1.0 mm), hair-brown underside, and distinct tornal green scales.

choices.label: Tail ~2.5 mm; underside pale purplish-brown; no/vestigial tornal green scales (montane)
Tail ~1.0 mm (very short); underside hair-brown; tornal green scales present

r_paralea.name: Arhopala paralea
r_paralea.note: Tail 2.5 mm. Underside ground colour pale purplish-brown; tornal green scales absent or vestigial. Male: violet-blue; FW border 2.0 mm at mid-costa, slightly increasing toward tornus and apex; HW border 2.5 mm. Montane on the Malay Peninsula.

r_buddha_cooperi.name: Arhopala buddha cooperi
r_buddha_cooperi.note: Tail very short ~1.0 mm. Underside ground colour hair-brown; tornal green scales present and distinct. Male: shining violet-blue; black borders 1.0 mm wide. Female: may resemble female A. aurea but differs in narrower HW border and thinner tail.

r_arvina_adalitas.name: Arhopala arvina adalitas
r_arvina_adalitas.note: Tailless. Underside ground colour hair-brown; tornal green scales present. Male: darker blue than A. buddha; black border reduced to a thread. Female: purple-blue.

r_vihara.name: Arhopala vihara
r_vihara.note: Tailed. FW 20–22 mm. FW underside spot 4 in line with spots 5–6 (distinguishes from eumolphus group where spot 4 is out of line); no costal spot in FW space 10. HW spot 6 rounded, more or less separated; HW band only partially dislocated at vein 2. Male: dark violet-blue; border slightly less than 1.0 mm. Female: bluer shade. Two ssp.: vihara on the peninsular mainland; hirava on Langkawi.

r_muta_all_unclear.name: Muta subgroup — tropaea / moorei / muta (unresolved)
r_muta_all_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without male upperside colour and size.

A. tropaea: notably small; male duller bluish-purple. Underside markings more quadrate.

A. moorei busa: larger (FW 16–19 mm); male bright blue; FW and HW same shade of blue in all lights.

A. muta maranda: larger (FW 16–19 mm); male bright blue; FW dark bright blue, HW slightly paler and more glistening; border slightly broader.

Inspect the male upperside to confirm species.

r_muta_s2_unclear.name: Muta subgroup — moorei or muta maranda (unresolved)
r_muta_s2_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without male upperside.

A. moorei busa: FW and HW exactly the same shade of bright blue in all lights; female more purple with broader borders.

A. muta maranda: FW dark bright blue, HW slightly paler and more glistening; borders slightly broader than moorei.

Inspect the male upperside in multiple light angles to confirm species.

r_camdeo_incomplete_unclear.name: Camdeo group — belphoebe or johoreana (unresolved)
r_camdeo_incomplete_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without upperside and tail inspection.

A. belphoebe cowani: tailless; broad dark borders; pale violet-blue; both sexes alike.

A. johoreana johoreana: short tail present; much darker violet-blue.

Check hindwing for tail presence/absence to confirm species.

r_alea_s6_unclear.name: Alea subgroup — phaenops / normani / sublustris (unresolved)
r_alea_s6_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot determine to species without male border pattern.

A. phaenops sandakani: male border expands notably toward apex; HW spot 6 does not overlap end-cell bar.

A. normani: male less shining, more violet than blue; borders narrow. ⚠ Requires genitalia dissection to confirm vs sublustris.

A. sublustris ridleyi: bright glistening blue; borders under 0.5 mm. ⚠ Requires genitalia dissection to confirm vs normani.

Inspect the male upperside border width and sheen to narrow further.

r_alea_glist_unclear.name: Alea subgroup — normani or sublustris (unresolved)
r_alea_glist_unclear.note:
⚠ Cannot distinguish to species without male upperside sheen and colour.

A. normani: male less shining, more violet than bright blue.

A. sublustris ridleyi (Ridley's Oakblue): male bright glistening blue; borders under 0.5 mm.

⚠ Note: both species may also require male genitalia dissection for definitive identification. Inspect male upperside sheen to differentiate.
